{{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '宮城県仙台市太白区富沢3-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '宮城県名取市高柳', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:多賀神社 (宮城県名取郡)]] {{wikidata link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''多賀神社'''（たがじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|宮城県|Q47896}}旧{{jalink|名取郡|Q6980219}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 {{jalink|陸奥国|Q907389}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式}}{{jalink|式内社}}百座のうち{{jalink|名取郡|Q6980219}}にある二座のうちの一座「多加神社」であり、現在{{jalink|仙台市|Q46747}}{{jalink|太白区|Q1043331}}富沢と{{jalink|名取市|Q642018}}高柳に鎮座する二社が論社とされている。 === 多賀神社（仙台市太白区富沢） === {{神社 |名称=多賀神社（仙台市太白区） |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|多賀神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|宮城県|Q47896}}{{jalink|仙台市|Q46747}}{{jalink|太白区|Q1043331}}{{jalink|富沢 (仙台市)||富沢}}3-15-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=多賀神社（仙台市太白区）}} |祭神={{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}} |社格=旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}
{{jalink|式内社}} |創建={{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}40年（{{jalink|110年|Q30631}}） |本殿=春日造 |別名=大鷹宮 |例祭=5月1日 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=多賀神社|coordinates= {{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === 祭神 === * {{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}}を主祭神として祀る。 * その他に、大野田地区の{{jalink|春日神社|Q10855011}}（祭神：{{jalink|アメノコヤネ||天児屋命}}）と{{jalink|寶龍神社}}（祭神：{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高龗神}}）を合祀している。 ===由緒=== 多賀神社の鎮座する地域には富沢遺跡・大野田遺跡などの縄文時代の遺跡や大野田古墳群、{{jalink|郡山遺跡|Q11643209}}があるなど、歴史的にも重要な地域であった。 多賀神社は'''仙台市内で一番古い神社'''であり、社伝によれば、景行天皇40年（110年）に{{jalink|日本武尊}}が東夷征伐の時に勧請したという。第21代{{jalink|雄略天皇|Q357235}}2年（{{jalink|458年|Q30922}}）には圭田58束を賜り神礼祭式を行ったと伝わる。往古は「大鷹宮」と呼ばれていたという。 境内由緒書によると、多賀神社はこの地方の守護神として領主や武将から篤く崇敬されたという。第70代{{jalink|後冷泉天皇|Q356060}}の御代には、鎮守府将軍である{{jalink|源義家|Q32613}}が東征の折に{{jalink|名取川|Q11414763}}を渡り当社に参拝し、武運長久を祈願し自筆の物を奉納したという。 {{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}年間に建ててあった仮宮が大破。その後も歴代仙台藩主から崇敬されたが、{{jalink|伊達重村|Q846378}}の寄進を受け、{{jalink|安永}}4年（{{jalink|1775年|Q7700}}）に社殿の普請が行われた。角田城主であった{{jalink|石川宗光|Q11585764}}からも「多賀神社伊弉諾尊」の神号献上を受け、奥方の代参や侍女の参詣で神社も賑わったと伝わる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年4月に村社に指定、同42年には大野田地区の春日神社と東大野田地区の寶龍神社を合祀し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}8年の8月には幣帛供進社に指定された。 ===境内=== *社殿（拝殿・幣殿・春日造の本殿） *その他に、石碑や石祠、箍納め所、御神木の{{jalink|カシワ|Q1377319|柏}}の木、神楽殿、社務所がある。 shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|秋の境内 File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|秋の境内 File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|秋の境内 File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|多賀神社の御神木「柏の木」 File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|多賀神社拝殿前 File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|多賀神社拝殿 File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai ciｔy.JPG|神前に奉納されている箍（たが） File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|春日造の多賀神社本殿 File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|多賀神社境内の石祠
末社の山田社、兒社、荒神社、鈎取社、姪子社、伊勢宮、日向社を祀る File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|箍納め所
祈願をこめた箍（たが）が奉納されている {{-}} ===多賀神社（名取市高柳）=== {{神社 |名称=多賀神社（名取市高柳） |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|多賀神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|宮城県|Q47896}}{{jalink|名取市|Q642018}}{{jalink|高柳 (名取市)||高柳}}字{{jalink|高柳下西||下西}}50 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=多賀神社（名取市高柳）}} |祭神={{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}}
{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊弉冉尊}} |社格=旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|式内社}} |創建={{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}28年（{{jalink|98年|Q30575}}） |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名=名取総鎮守 |例祭=5月1日 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=多賀神社|coordinates= {{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} ===祭神=== *{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}}と{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊弉冉尊}}を主祭神として祀る。 *その他に、近隣の神社の祭神である熊野加夫呂櫛御食奴神、{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照皇大神}}、{{jalink|スサノヲ||速須佐男命}}、{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}を合祀している。 ===由緒=== 景行天皇28年2月に日本武尊による東征の折に勧請されたと伝わる。雄略天皇2年(458年)11月に祭祀田として圭田58束を奉り祭礼を行ったという。当社の東には、その折に賜った祭祀田にちなむ「圭田」という地名が残されている。 神社の由緒によれば、日本武尊が東征のために奥州へやってきた時、相次ぐ戦いや長旅によって重い病気を患ってしまった。その時、柳の生い茂る温暖な地で祭壇や祠を設けて病気平癒を祈願したところ回復し、無事に大和の地へ帰ることができたという。その病気平癒や国家安寧を祈願した祠が「多賀神社」のはじまりだと伝わる。 当社の鎮座する地域にはこの由緒にちなむ地域名が現在も残されている。 *多賀神社の北には日本武尊が戦勝祈願のための祭壇を設けた場所である「皇壇原（こうだんがはら）」 *柳の木で祭壇を作り病気平癒を祈願した場所である「'''高柳'''（たなやなぎ（棚柳）→たかやなぎ）」（現在の鎮座地名） また、当社の南東970メートルの民家敷地内には日本武尊の神霊を祀る「白鳥神社」が鎮座している。 File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|白鳥神社境内 File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|白鳥神社 File:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|白鳥神社本殿 当社は仙台市太白区富沢の多賀神社との間で、式内社「多加神社」がどちらの神社であるかという論争が度々起こったという。歴史書「新撰陸奥風土記」には『多賀神社は富沢村にあり今は高柳村に鎮座するという。しかし、高柳の多賀神社は往古は「若宮八幡」と呼ばれていたものを多賀神社へ改称したものである』と書かれているとある。明治22年にも論争が起こり、それにより富沢の多賀神社を式内社、高柳の多賀神社は祭祀田などの社領のあった地だとした。太白区の「'''{{jalink|西多賀村|Q11627540|西多賀}}'''」の地名は富沢の多賀神社にちなむもので、閖上地区や高柳の当社付近は以前は「'''{{jalink|閖上町|Q11655485|東多賀}}'''」と呼ばれていた。 明治4年2月には郷社になり、その後幣帛供進社に指定された。藩主や陸奥国守からも尊崇を集めていた。日本武尊の由緒にちなみ、特に病気平癒、延命長寿に御利益がある神社として近隣地域のみならず他県からも多くの崇敬者を集めている。 ===境内=== *社殿（拝殿・幣殿・流造の本殿） *境内には御神門、社務所がある。 File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|多賀神社鳥居 File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|多賀神社御神門 File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|多賀神社拝殿 File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|多賀神社拝殿に奉納されている箍(たが) File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|流造の多賀神社本殿 File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|多賀神社本殿 {{clear|right}} === 参考文献 === *多賀神社由緒（大正3年8月） === [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 多賀神社（名取市高柳）]（宮城県{{jalink|神社庁|Q11590703}}） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=宮城県}} == Categories {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '宮城県仙台市太白区富沢3-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '宮城県名取市高柳', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:多賀神社 (宮城県名取郡)]] {{wikidata link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''多賀神社'''（たがじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|宮城県|Q47896}}旧{{jalink|名取郡|Q6980219}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 {{jalink|陸奥国|Q907389}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式}}{{jalink|式内社}}百座のうち{{jalink|名取郡|Q6980219}}にある二座のうちの一座「多加神社」であり、現在{{jalink|仙台市|Q46747}}{{jalink|太白区|Q1043331}}富沢と{{jalink|名取市|Q642018}}高柳に鎮座する二社が論社とされている。 === 多賀神社（仙台市太白区富沢） === {{神社 |名称=多賀神社（仙台市太白区） |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|多賀神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|宮城県|Q47896}}{{jalink|仙台市|Q46747}}{{jalink|太白区|Q1043331}}{{jalink|富沢 (仙台市)||富沢}}3-15-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=多賀神社（仙台市太白区）}} |祭神={{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}} |社格=旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}
{{jalink|式内社}} |創建={{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}40年（{{jalink|110年|Q30631}}） |本殿=春日造 |別名=大鷹宮 |例祭=5月1日 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=多賀神社|coordinates= {{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === 祭神 === * {{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}}を主祭神として祀る。 * その他に、大野田地区の{{jalink|春日神社|Q10855011}}（祭神：{{jalink|アメノコヤネ||天児屋命}}）と{{jalink|寶龍神社}}（祭神：{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高龗神}}）を合祀している。 ===由緒=== 多賀神社の鎮座する地域には富沢遺跡・大野田遺跡などの縄文時代の遺跡や大野田古墳群、{{jalink|郡山遺跡|Q11643209}}があるなど、歴史的にも重要な地域であった。 多賀神社は'''仙台市内で一番古い神社'''であり、社伝によれば、景行天皇40年（110年）に{{jalink|日本武尊}}が東夷征伐の時に勧請したという。第21代{{jalink|雄略天皇|Q357235}}2年（{{jalink|458年|Q30922}}）には圭田58束を賜り神礼祭式を行ったと伝わる。往古は「大鷹宮」と呼ばれていたという。 境内由緒書によると、多賀神社はこの地方の守護神として領主や武将から篤く崇敬されたという。第70代{{jalink|後冷泉天皇|Q356060}}の御代には、鎮守府将軍である{{jalink|源義家|Q32613}}が東征の折に{{jalink|名取川|Q11414763}}を渡り当社に参拝し、武運長久を祈願し自筆の物を奉納したという。 {{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}年間に建ててあった仮宮が大破。その後も歴代仙台藩主から崇敬されたが、{{jalink|伊達重村|Q846378}}の寄進を受け、{{jalink|安永}}4年（{{jalink|1775年|Q7700}}）に社殿の普請が行われた。角田城主であった{{jalink|石川宗光|Q11585764}}からも「多賀神社伊弉諾尊」の神号献上を受け、奥方の代参や侍女の参詣で神社も賑わったと伝わる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年4月に村社に指定、同42年には大野田地区の春日神社と東大野田地区の寶龍神社を合祀し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}8年の8月には幣帛供進社に指定された。 ===境内=== *社殿（拝殿・幣殿・春日造の本殿） *その他に、石碑や石祠、箍納め所、御神木の{{jalink|カシワ|Q1377319|柏}}の木、神楽殿、社務所がある。 shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|秋の境内 File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|秋の境内 File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|秋の境内 File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|多賀神社の御神木「柏の木」 File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|多賀神社拝殿前 File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|多賀神社拝殿 File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai ciｔy.JPG|神前に奉納されている箍（たが） File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|春日造の多賀神社本殿 File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|多賀神社境内の石祠
末社の山田社、兒社、荒神社、鈎取社、姪子社、伊勢宮、日向社を祀る File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|箍納め所
祈願をこめた箍（たが）が奉納されている {{-}} ===多賀神社（名取市高柳）=== {{神社 |名称=多賀神社（名取市高柳） |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|多賀神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|宮城県|Q47896}}{{jalink|名取市|Q642018}}{{jalink|高柳 (名取市)||高柳}}字{{jalink|高柳下西||下西}}50 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=多賀神社（名取市高柳）}} |祭神={{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}}
{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊弉冉尊}} |社格=旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|式内社}} |創建={{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}28年（{{jalink|98年|Q30575}}） |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名=名取総鎮守 |例祭=5月1日 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=多賀神社|coordinates= {{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} ===祭神=== *{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}}と{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊弉冉尊}}を主祭神として祀る。 *その他に、近隣の神社の祭神である熊野加夫呂櫛御食奴神、{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照皇大神}}、{{jalink|スサノヲ||速須佐男命}}、{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}を合祀している。 ===由緒=== 景行天皇28年2月に日本武尊による東征の折に勧請されたと伝わる。雄略天皇2年(458年)11月に祭祀田として圭田58束を奉り祭礼を行ったという。当社の東には、その折に賜った祭祀田にちなむ「圭田」という地名が残されている。 神社の由緒によれば、日本武尊が東征のために奥州へやってきた時、相次ぐ戦いや長旅によって重い病気を患ってしまった。その時、柳の生い茂る温暖な地で祭壇や祠を設けて病気平癒を祈願したところ回復し、無事に大和の地へ帰ることができたという。その病気平癒や国家安寧を祈願した祠が「多賀神社」のはじまりだと伝わる。 当社の鎮座する地域にはこの由緒にちなむ地域名が現在も残されている。 *多賀神社の北には日本武尊が戦勝祈願のための祭壇を設けた場所である「皇壇原（こうだんがはら）」 *柳の木で祭壇を作り病気平癒を祈願した場所である「'''高柳'''（たなやなぎ（棚柳）→たかやなぎ）」（現在の鎮座地名） また、当社の南東970メートルの民家敷地内には日本武尊の神霊を祀る「白鳥神社」が鎮座している。 File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|白鳥神社境内 File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|白鳥神社 File:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|白鳥神社本殿 当社は仙台市太白区富沢の多賀神社との間で、式内社「多加神社」がどちらの神社であるかという論争が度々起こったという。歴史書「新撰陸奥風土記」には『多賀神社は富沢村にあり今は高柳村に鎮座するという。しかし、高柳の多賀神社は往古は「若宮八幡」と呼ばれていたものを多賀神社へ改称したものである』と書かれているとある。明治22年にも論争が起こり、それにより富沢の多賀神社を式内社、高柳の多賀神社は祭祀田などの社領のあった地だとした。太白区の「'''{{jalink|西多賀村|Q11627540|西多賀}}'''」の地名は富沢の多賀神社にちなむもので、閖上地区や高柳の当社付近は以前は「'''{{jalink|閖上町|Q11655485|東多賀}}'''」と呼ばれていた。 明治4年2月には郷社になり、その後幣帛供進社に指定された。藩主や陸奥国守からも尊崇を集めていた。日本武尊の由緒にちなみ、特に病気平癒、延命長寿に御利益がある神社として近隣地域のみならず他県からも多くの崇敬者を集めている。 ===境内=== *社殿（拝殿・幣殿・流造の本殿） *境内には御神門、社務所がある。 File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|多賀神社鳥居 File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|多賀神社御神門 File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|多賀神社拝殿 File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|多賀神社拝殿に奉納されている箍(たが) File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|流造の多賀神社本殿 File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|多賀神社本殿 {{clear|right}} === 参考文献 === *多賀神社由緒（大正3年8月） === {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '宮城県仙台市太白区富沢3-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '宮城県名取市高柳', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:多賀神社 (宮城県名取郡)]] {{wikidata link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''Taga Shrine''' (たがじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in former {{jalink|Natori District|Q6980219}}, {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}. It corresponds to “Taka Shrine,” one of the two shrines in Natori District among the 100 {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' and two current shrines are treated as claim shrines: one in Tomizawa, {{jalink|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Q1043331}}, and one in Takayanagi, {{jalink|Natori|Q642018}}. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku-ku, Sendai) === {{神社 |名称=Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Worship hall of Taga Shrine]]
Worship hall |所在地={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Sendai|Q46747}}{{jalink|Taihaku-ku|Q1043331}}{{jalink|Tomizawa (Sendai)||Tomizawa}} 3-15-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai)}} |祭神={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} |社格=former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} 40 ({{jalink|110|Q30631}}) |本殿=Kasuga-zukuri |別名=Ōtaka-no-miya |例祭=May 1 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deity === * Main deity: {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}. * Also merged are {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} of the Ōnoda area (deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}) and Hōryū Shrine (deity: {{jalink|Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|Takaokami-no-Kami}}). === History === The area where Taga Shrine stands was historically important, containing Jōmon sites such as the Tomizawa and Ōnoda sites, the Ōnoda kofun cluster, and {{jalink|Kōriyama site|Q11643209}}. Taga Shrine is said to be '''the oldest shrine in Sendai'''; according to shrine tradition, it was invited by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} in {{jalink|110|Q30631}} (Keikō 40) during his eastern campaign. In year 2 of the 21st emperor {{jalink|Yūryaku|Q357235}} ({{jalink|458|Q30922}}), it is said to have received 58 bundles of ritual rice field and held divine rites. In ancient times it was reportedly called “Ōtaka-no-miya.” According to the precinct history board, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by local lords and warriors as guardian deity of this region. In the reign of the 70th emperor {{jalink|Go-Reizei|Q356060}}, Chinju-fu shōgun {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}} crossed the {{jalink|Natori River|Q11414763}} during eastern campaign, worshipped here, prayed for lasting military fortune, and dedicated his own handwritten item. A temporary shrine built in the {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} era was heavily damaged. Reverence by successive Sendai lords continued, and with donation from {{jalink|Date Shigemura|Q846378}}, shrine construction was carried out in {{jalink|An'ei}} 4 ({{jalink|1775|Q7700}}). It is also said that {{jalink|Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764}}, lord of Kakuda Castle, offered the divine title “Taga Jinja Izanagi-no-Mikoto,” and the shrine was lively with visits by his wife’s representative and ladies-in-waiting. It was designated Village Shrine in April {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5, merged Kasuga Shrine in Ōnoda and Hōryū Shrine in Higashi-Ōnoda in Meiji 42, and in August {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 8 was designated a heihaku offering shrine. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings (worship hall, heiden, Kasuga-zukuri main sanctuary) * Also stone monuments, stone shrines, hoop-offering place, sacred {{jalink|oak|Q1377319|kashiwa}} tree, kagura hall, and shrine office. File:1st Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 01.JPG|First torii of Taga Shrine File:2nd Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Second torii of Taga Shrine File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Sacred "oak tree" of Taga Shrine File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|In front of worship hall File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|Worship hall File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai ciｔy.JPG|Hoops (taga) dedicated before the deity File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Kasuga-zukuri main sanctuary File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Stone shrine in precincts
Enshrines massha: Yamada-sha, Ko-sha, Kōjin-sha, Kagitori-sha, Meiko-sha, Ise-miya, Hyūga-sha File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|Hoop-offering place
Prayer hoops are dedicated {{-}} === Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori) === {{神社 |名称=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori) |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Worship hall of Taga Shrine]]
Worship hall |所在地={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Natori|Q642018}}{{jalink|Takayanagi (Natori)||Takayanagi}} Aza {{jalink|Shimonishi||Shimonishi}} 50 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori)}} |祭神={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} |社格=former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} 28 ({{jalink|98|Q30575}}) |本殿={{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名=General tutelary of Natori |例祭=May 1 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}}. * Also merged: Kumano Kafuro Kushi Mikenuno-no-Kami, {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} from nearby shrines. === History === Tradition says it was invited by Yamato Takeru in February {{jalink|98|Q30575}} (Keikō 28) during eastern campaign. In November {{jalink|458|Q30922}} (Yūryaku 2), 58 bundles of ritual fields were dedicated and festivals performed. East of the shrine remains the place name “Keiden,” said to derive from those ritual fields. According to shrine history, when Yamato Takeru came to Ōshū for eastern campaign, he fell seriously ill due to successive battles and long travel. In a warm place thick with willows, he built an altar and shrine and prayed for healing, recovered, and safely returned to Yamato. The shrine where those prayers for healing and national peace were made is said to be the origin of Taga Shrine. Place names related to this tradition still remain: * North of Taga Shrine is “Kōdangahara,” where Yamato Takeru set an altar for victory prayers. * “'''Takayanagi'''” (tanayanagi/tanayagi → takayanagi), where a willow altar was made and healing prayed for (current shrine area name). Also, 970 meters southeast of this shrine, on private residential land, stands “Shiratori Shrine” enshrining the spirit of Yamato Takeru. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine precincts File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine File:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine main sanctuary It is said there were frequent disputes with Taga Shrine in Tomizawa (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) over which shrine was the Shikinaisha “Taka Shrine.” The historical work 'Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki' is cited as saying: “Taga Shrine is in Tomizawa Village and now is said to be in Takayanagi Village. However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was formerly called Wakamiya Hachiman and was renamed Taga Shrine.” A dispute also occurred in Meiji 22, resulting in Tomizawa Taga Shrine being treated as the Shikinaisha and Takayanagi Taga Shrine as the site with ritual fields and shrine lands. The place name “'''{{jalink|Nishitaga|Q11627540|Nishitaga}}'''” in Taihaku-ku derives from the Tomizawa shrine, while the Yuriage area and around this shrine in Takayanagi were formerly called “'''{{jalink|Higashitaga|Q11655485|Higashitaga}}'''." In February Meiji 4 it became Gōsha, and later was designated a heihaku offering shrine. It was revered by domain lords and governors of Mutsu. Owing to the Yamato Takeru tradition, it is especially believed beneficial for healing illness and longevity, attracting worshippers not only locally but also from other prefectures. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings (worship hall, heiden, nagare-zukuri main sanctuary) * Precincts also include sacred gate and shrine office. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Taga Shrine torii File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Sacred gate File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Worship hall File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Hoops (taga) dedicated at worship hall File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagare-zukuri main sanctuary File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Main sanctuary {{clear|right}} === Bibliography === *Taga Shrine history (August, Taishō 3) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai)] (Miyagi {{jalink|Shrine Office|Q11590703}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori)] (Miyagi {{jalink|Shrine Office|Q11590703}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=宮城県}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''Taga Shrine''' (たがじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in former {{jalink|Natori District|Q6980219}}, {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}. It corresponds to “Taka Shrine,” one of the two shrines in Natori District among the 100 {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' and two current shrines are treated as claim shrines: one in Tomizawa, {{jalink|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Q1043331}}, and one in Takayanagi, {{jalink|Natori|Q642018}}. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku-ku, Sendai) === {{神社 |名称=Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Worship hall of Taga Shrine]]
Worship hall |所在地={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Sendai|Q46747}}{{jalink|Taihaku-ku|Q1043331}}{{jalink|Tomizawa (Sendai)||Tomizawa}} 3-15-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai)}} |祭神={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} |社格=former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} 40 ({{jalink|110|Q30631}}) |本殿=Kasuga-zukuri |別名=Ōtaka-no-miya |例祭=May 1 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deity === * Main deity: {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}. * Also merged are {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} of the Ōnoda area (deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}) and Hōryū Shrine (deity: {{jalink|Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|Takaokami-no-Kami}}). === History === The area where Taga Shrine stands was historically important, containing Jōmon sites such as the Tomizawa and Ōnoda sites, the Ōnoda kofun cluster, and {{jalink|Kōriyama site|Q11643209}}. Taga Shrine is said to be '''the oldest shrine in Sendai'''; according to shrine tradition, it was invited by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} in {{jalink|110|Q30631}} (Keikō 40) during his eastern campaign. In year 2 of the 21st emperor {{jalink|Yūryaku|Q357235}} ({{jalink|458|Q30922}}), it is said to have received 58 bundles of ritual rice field and held divine rites. In ancient times it was reportedly called “Ōtaka-no-miya.” According to the precinct history board, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by local lords and warriors as guardian deity of this region. In the reign of the 70th emperor {{jalink|Go-Reizei|Q356060}}, Chinju-fu shōgun {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}} crossed the {{jalink|Natori River|Q11414763}} during eastern campaign, worshipped here, prayed for lasting military fortune, and dedicated his own handwritten item. A temporary shrine built in the {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} era was heavily damaged. Reverence by successive Sendai lords continued, and with donation from {{jalink|Date Shigemura|Q846378}}, shrine construction was carried out in {{jalink|An'ei}} 4 ({{jalink|1775|Q7700}}). It is also said that {{jalink|Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764}}, lord of Kakuda Castle, offered the divine title “Taga Jinja Izanagi-no-Mikoto,” and the shrine was lively with visits by his wife’s representative and ladies-in-waiting. It was designated Village Shrine in April {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5, merged Kasuga Shrine in Ōnoda and Hōryū Shrine in Higashi-Ōnoda in Meiji 42, and in August {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 8 was designated a heihaku offering shrine. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings (worship hall, heiden, Kasuga-zukuri main sanctuary) * Also stone monuments, stone shrines, hoop-offering place, sacred {{jalink|oak|Q1377319|kashiwa}} tree, kagura hall, and shrine office. File:1st Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 01.JPG|First torii of Taga Shrine File:2nd Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Second torii of Taga Shrine File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Sacred "oak tree" of Taga Shrine File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|In front of worship hall File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|Worship hall File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai ciｔy.JPG|Hoops (taga) dedicated before the deity File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Kasuga-zukuri main sanctuary File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Stone shrine in precincts
Enshrines massha: Yamada-sha, Ko-sha, Kōjin-sha, Kagitori-sha, Meiko-sha, Ise-miya, Hyūga-sha File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|Hoop-offering place
Prayer hoops are dedicated {{-}} === Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori) === {{神社 |名称=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori) |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Worship hall of Taga Shrine]]
Worship hall |所在地={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Natori|Q642018}}{{jalink|Takayanagi (Natori)||Takayanagi}} Aza {{jalink|Shimonishi||Shimonishi}} 50 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori)}} |祭神={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} |社格=former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} 28 ({{jalink|98|Q30575}}) |本殿={{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名=General tutelary of Natori |例祭=May 1 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}}. * Also merged: Kumano Kafuro Kushi Mikenuno-no-Kami, {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} from nearby shrines. === History === Tradition says it was invited by Yamato Takeru in February {{jalink|98|Q30575}} (Keikō 28) during eastern campaign. In November {{jalink|458|Q30922}} (Yūryaku 2), 58 bundles of ritual fields were dedicated and festivals performed. East of the shrine remains the place name “Keiden,” said to derive from those ritual fields. According to shrine history, when Yamato Takeru came to Ōshū for eastern campaign, he fell seriously ill due to successive battles and long travel. In a warm place thick with willows, he built an altar and shrine and prayed for healing, recovered, and safely returned to Yamato. The shrine where those prayers for healing and national peace were made is said to be the origin of Taga Shrine. Place names related to this tradition still remain: * North of Taga Shrine is “Kōdangahara,” where Yamato Takeru set an altar for victory prayers. * “'''Takayanagi'''” (tanayanagi/tanayagi → takayanagi), where a willow altar was made and healing prayed for (current shrine area name). Also, 970 meters southeast of this shrine, on private residential land, stands “Shiratori Shrine” enshrining the spirit of Yamato Takeru. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine precincts File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine File:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine main sanctuary It is said there were frequent disputes with Taga Shrine in Tomizawa (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) over which shrine was the Shikinaisha “Taka Shrine.” The historical work 'Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki' is cited as saying: “Taga Shrine is in Tomizawa Village and now is said to be in Takayanagi Village. However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was formerly called Wakamiya Hachiman and was renamed Taga Shrine.” A dispute also occurred in Meiji 22, resulting in Tomizawa Taga Shrine being treated as the Shikinaisha and Takayanagi Taga Shrine as the site with ritual fields and shrine lands. The place name “'''{{jalink|Nishitaga|Q11627540|Nishitaga}}'''” in Taihaku-ku derives from the Tomizawa shrine, while the Yuriage area and around this shrine in Takayanagi were formerly called “'''{{jalink|Higashitaga|Q11655485|Higashitaga}}'''." In February Meiji 4 it became Gōsha, and later was designated a heihaku offering shrine. It was revered by domain lords and governors of Mutsu. Owing to the Yamato Takeru tradition, it is especially believed beneficial for healing illness and longevity, attracting worshippers not only locally but also from other prefectures. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings (worship hall, heiden, nagare-zukuri main sanctuary) * Precincts also include sacred gate and shrine office. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Taga Shrine torii File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Sacred gate File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Worship hall File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Hoops (taga) dedicated at worship hall File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagare-zukuri main sanctuary File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Main sanctuary {{clear|right}} === Bibliography === *Taga Shrine history (August, Taishō 3) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai)] (Miyagi {{jalink|Shrine Office|Q11590703}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori)] (Miyagi {{jalink|Shrine Office|Q11590703}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=宮城県}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''Taga Shrine''' (たがじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in former {{jalink|Natori District|Q6980219}}, {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}. It corresponds to “Taka Shrine,” one of the two shrines in Natori District among the 100 {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' and two current shrines are treated as claim shrines: one in Tomizawa, {{jalink|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Q1043331}}, and one in Takayanagi, {{jalink|Natori|Q642018}}. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku-ku, Sendai) === {{神社 |名称=Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Worship hall of Taga Shrine]]
Worship hall |所在地={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Sendai|Q46747}}{{jalink|Taihaku-ku|Q1043331}}{{jalink|Tomizawa (Sendai)||Tomizawa}} 3-15-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai)}} |祭神={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} |社格=former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} 40 ({{jalink|110|Q30631}}) |本殿=Kasuga-zukuri |別名=Ōtaka-no-miya |例祭=May 1 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deity === * Main deity: {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}. * Also merged are {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} of the Ōnoda area (deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}) and Hōryū Shrine (deity: {{jalink|Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|Takaokami-no-Kami}}). === History === The area where Taga Shrine stands was historically important, containing Jōmon sites such as the Tomizawa and Ōnoda sites, the Ōnoda kofun cluster, and {{jalink|Kōriyama site|Q11643209}}. Taga Shrine is said to be '''the oldest shrine in Sendai'''; according to shrine tradition, it was invited by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} in {{jalink|110|Q30631}} (Keikō 40) during his eastern campaign. In year 2 of the 21st emperor {{jalink|Yūryaku|Q357235}} ({{jalink|458|Q30922}}), it is said to have received 58 bundles of ritual rice field and held divine rites. In ancient times it was reportedly called “Ōtaka-no-miya.” According to the precinct history board, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by local lords and warriors as guardian deity of this region. In the reign of the 70th emperor {{jalink|Go-Reizei|Q356060}}, Chinju-fu shōgun {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}} crossed the {{jalink|Natori River|Q11414763}} during eastern campaign, worshipped here, prayed for lasting military fortune, and dedicated his own handwritten item. A temporary shrine built in the {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} era was heavily damaged. Reverence by successive Sendai lords continued, and with donation from {{jalink|Date Shigemura|Q846378}}, shrine construction was carried out in {{jalink|An'ei}} 4 ({{jalink|1775|Q7700}}). It is also said that {{jalink|Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764}}, lord of Kakuda Castle, offered the divine title “Taga Jinja Izanagi-no-Mikoto,” and the shrine was lively with visits by his wife’s representative and ladies-in-waiting. It was designated Village Shrine in April {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5, merged Kasuga Shrine in Ōnoda and Hōryū Shrine in Higashi-Ōnoda in Meiji 42, and in August {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 8 was designated a heihaku offering shrine. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings (worship hall, heiden, Kasuga-zukuri main sanctuary) * Also stone monuments, stone shrines, hoop-offering place, sacred {{jalink|oak|Q1377319|kashiwa}} tree, kagura hall, and shrine office. File:1st Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 01.JPG|First torii of Taga Shrine File:2nd Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Second torii of Taga Shrine File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Sacred "oak tree" of Taga Shrine File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|In front of worship hall File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|Worship hall File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai ciｔy.JPG|Hoops (taga) dedicated before the deity File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Kasuga-zukuri main sanctuary File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Stone shrine in precincts
Enshrines massha: Yamada-sha, Ko-sha, Kōjin-sha, Kagitori-sha, Meiko-sha, Ise-miya, Hyūga-sha File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|Hoop-offering place
Prayer hoops are dedicated {{-}} === Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori) === {{神社 |名称=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori) |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Worship hall of Taga Shrine]]
Worship hall |所在地={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Natori|Q642018}}{{jalink|Takayanagi (Natori)||Takayanagi}} Aza {{jalink|Shimonishi||Shimonishi}} 50 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori)}} |祭神={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} |社格=former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} 28 ({{jalink|98|Q30575}}) |本殿={{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名=General tutelary of Natori |例祭=May 1 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}}. * Also merged: Kumano Kafuro Kushi Mikenuno-no-Kami, {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} from nearby shrines. === History === Tradition says it was invited by Yamato Takeru in February {{jalink|98|Q30575}} (Keikō 28) during eastern campaign. In November {{jalink|458|Q30922}} (Yūryaku 2), 58 bundles of ritual fields were dedicated and festivals performed. East of the shrine remains the place name “Keiden,” said to derive from those ritual fields. According to shrine history, when Yamato Takeru came to Ōshū for eastern campaign, he fell seriously ill due to successive battles and long travel. In a warm place thick with willows, he built an altar and shrine and prayed for healing, recovered, and safely returned to Yamato. The shrine where those prayers for healing and national peace were made is said to be the origin of Taga Shrine. Place names related to this tradition still remain: * North of Taga Shrine is “Kōdangahara,” where Yamato Takeru set an altar for victory prayers. * “'''Takayanagi'''” (tanayanagi/tanayagi → takayanagi), where a willow altar was made and healing prayed for (current shrine area name). Also, 970 meters southeast of this shrine, on private residential land, stands “Shiratori Shrine” enshrining the spirit of Yamato Takeru. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine precincts File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine File:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine main sanctuary It is said there were frequent disputes with Taga Shrine in Tomizawa (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) over which shrine was the Shikinaisha “Taka Shrine.” The historical work 'Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki' is cited as saying: “Taga Shrine is in Tomizawa Village and now is said to be in Takayanagi Village. However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was formerly called Wakamiya Hachiman and was renamed Taga Shrine.” A dispute also occurred in Meiji 22, resulting in Tomizawa Taga Shrine being treated as the Shikinaisha and Takayanagi Taga Shrine as the site with ritual fields and shrine lands. The place name “'''{{jalink|Nishitaga|Q11627540|Nishitaga}}'''” in Taihaku-ku derives from the Tomizawa shrine, while the Yuriage area and around this shrine in Takayanagi were formerly called “'''{{jalink|Higashitaga|Q11655485|Higashitaga}}'''." In February Meiji 4 it became Gōsha, and later was designated a heihaku offering shrine. It was revered by domain lords and governors of Mutsu. Owing to the Yamato Takeru tradition, it is especially believed beneficial for healing illness and longevity, attracting worshippers not only locally but also from other prefectures. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings (worship hall, heiden, nagare-zukuri main sanctuary) * Precincts also include sacred gate and shrine office. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Taga Shrine torii File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Sacred gate File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Worship hall File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Hoops (taga) dedicated at worship hall File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagare-zukuri main sanctuary File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Main sanctuary {{clear|right}} === Bibliography === *Taga Shrine history (August, Taishō 3) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai)] (Miyagi {{jalink|Shrine Office|Q11590703}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori)] (Miyagi {{jalink|Shrine Office|Q11590703}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=宮城県}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

It corresponds to “Taka Shrine,” one of the two shrines in Natori District among the 100 {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' and two current shrines are treated as claim shrines: one in Tomizawa, {{jalink|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Q1043331}}, and one in Takayanagi, {{jalink|Natori|Q642018}}. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku-ku, Sendai) === {{神社 |名称=Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Worship hall of Taga Shrine]]

{{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} 40 ({{jalink|110|Q30631}}) |本殿=Kasuga-zukuri |別名=Ōtaka-no-miya |例祭=May 1 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deity === * Main deity: {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}. * Also merged are {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} of the Ōnoda area (deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}) and Hōryū Shrine (deity: {{jalink|Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|Takaokami-no-Kami}}). === History === The area where Taga Shrine stands was historically important, containing Jōmon sites such as the Tomizawa and Ōnoda sites, the Ōnoda kofun cluster, and {{jalink|Kōriyama site|Q11643209}}. Taga Shrine is said to be '''the oldest shrine in Sendai'''; according to shrine tradition, it was invited by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} in {{jalink|110|Q30631}} (Keikō 40) during his eastern campaign. In year 2 of the 21st emperor {{jalink|Yūryaku|Q357235}} ({{jalink|458|Q30922}}), it is said to have received 58 bundles of ritual rice field and held divine rites. In ancient times it was reportedly called “Ōtaka-no-miya.” According to the precinct history board, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by local lords and warriors as guardian deity of this region. In the reign of the 70th emperor {{jalink|Go-Reizei|Q356060}}, Chinju-fu shōgun {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}} crossed the {{jalink|Natori River|Q11414763}} during eastern campaign, worshipped here, prayed for lasting military fortune, and dedicated his own handwritten item. A temporary shrine built in the {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} era was heavily damaged. Reverence by successive Sendai lords continued, and with donation from {{jalink|Date Shigemura|Q846378}}, shrine construction was carried out in {{jalink|An'ei}} 4 ({{jalink|1775|Q7700}}). It is also said that {{jalink|Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764}}, lord of Kakuda Castle, offered the divine title “Taga Jinja Izanagi-no-Mikoto,” and the shrine was lively with visits by his wife’s representative and ladies-in-waiting. It was designated Village Shrine in April {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5, merged Kasuga Shrine in Ōnoda and Hōryū Shrine in Higashi-Ōnoda in Meiji 42, and in August {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 8 was designated a heihaku offering shrine. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings (worship hall, heiden, Kasuga-zukuri main sanctuary) * Also stone monuments, stone shrines, hoop-offering place, sacred {{jalink|oak|Q1377319|kashiwa}} tree, kagura hall, and shrine office. File:1st Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 01.JPG|First torii of Taga Shrine File:2nd Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Second torii of Taga Shrine File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Sacred "oak tree" of Taga Shrine File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|In front of worship hall File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|Worship hall File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai ciｔy.JPG|Hoops (taga) dedicated before the deity File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Kasuga-zukuri main sanctuary File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Stone shrine in precincts
Enshrines massha: Yamada-sha, Ko-sha, Kōjin-sha, Kagitori-sha, Meiko-sha, Ise-miya, Hyūga-sha File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|Hoop-offering place
Prayer hoops are dedicated {{-}} === Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori) === {{神社 |名称=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori) |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Worship hall of Taga Shrine]]
Worship hall |所在地={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Natori|Q642018}}{{jalink|Takayanagi (Natori)||Takayanagi}} Aza {{jalink|Shimonishi||Shimonishi}} 50 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori)}} |祭神={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} |社格=former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} 28 ({{jalink|98|Q30575}}) |本殿={{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名=General tutelary of Natori |例祭=May 1 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}}. * Also merged: Kumano Kafuro Kushi Mikenuno-no-Kami, {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} from nearby shrines. === History === Tradition says it was invited by Yamato Takeru in February {{jalink|98|Q30575}} (Keikō 28) during eastern campaign. In November {{jalink|458|Q30922}} (Yūryaku 2), 58 bundles of ritual fields were dedicated and festivals performed. East of the shrine remains the place name “Keiden,” said to derive from those ritual fields. According to shrine history, when Yamato Takeru came to Ōshū for eastern campaign, he fell seriously ill due to successive battles and long travel. In a warm place thick with willows, he built an altar and shrine and prayed for healing, recovered, and safely returned to Yamato. The shrine where those prayers for healing and national peace were made is said to be the origin of Taga Shrine. Place names related to this tradition still remain: * North of Taga Shrine is “Kōdangahara,” where Yamato Takeru set an altar for victory prayers. * “'''Takayanagi'''” (tanayanagi/tanayagi → takayanagi), where a willow altar was made and healing prayed for (current shrine area name). Also, 970 meters southeast of this shrine, on private residential land, stands “Shiratori Shrine” enshrining the spirit of Yamato Takeru. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine precincts File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine File:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine main sanctuary It is said there were frequent disputes with Taga Shrine in Tomizawa (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) over which shrine was the Shikinaisha “Taka Shrine.” The historical work 'Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki' is cited as saying: “Taga Shrine is in Tomizawa Village and now is said to be in Takayanagi Village. However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was formerly called Wakamiya Hachiman and was renamed Taga Shrine.” A dispute also occurred in Meiji 22, resulting in Tomizawa Taga Shrine being treated as the Shikinaisha and Takayanagi Taga Shrine as the site with ritual fields and shrine lands. The place name “'''{{jalink|Nishitaga|Q11627540|Nishitaga}}'''” in Taihaku-ku derives from the Tomizawa shrine, while the Yuriage area and around this shrine in Takayanagi were formerly called “'''{{jalink|Higashitaga|Q11655485|Higashitaga}}'''." In February Meiji 4 it became Gōsha, and later was designated a heihaku offering shrine. It was revered by domain lords and governors of Mutsu. Owing to the Yamato Takeru tradition, it is especially believed beneficial for healing illness and longevity, attracting worshippers not only locally but also from other prefectures. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings (worship hall, heiden, nagare-zukuri main sanctuary) * Precincts also include sacred gate and shrine office. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Taga Shrine torii File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Sacred gate File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Worship hall File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Hoops (taga) dedicated at worship hall File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagare-zukuri main sanctuary File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Main sanctuary {{clear|right}} === Bibliography === *Taga Shrine history (August, Taishō 3) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai)] (Miyagi {{jalink|Shrine Office|Q11590703}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori)] (Miyagi {{jalink|Shrine Office|Q11590703}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=宮城県}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is also said that {{jalink|Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764}}, lord of Kakuda Castle, offered the divine title “Taga Jinja Izanagi-no-Mikoto,” and the shrine was lively with visits by his wife’s representative and ladies-in-waiting. It was designated Village Shrine in April {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5, merged Kasuga Shrine in Ōnoda and Hōryū Shrine in Higashi-Ōnoda in Meiji 42, and in August {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 8 was designated a heihaku offering shrine. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings (worship hall, heiden, Kasuga-zukuri main sanctuary) * Also stone monuments, stone shrines, hoop-offering place, sacred {{jalink|oak|Q1377319|kashiwa}} tree, kagura hall, and shrine office. Prayer hoops are dedicated {{-}} === Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori) === {{神社 |名称=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori) |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Worship hall of Taga Shrine]]
Worship hall |所在地={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Natori|Q642018}}{{jalink|Takayanagi (Natori)||Takayanagi}} Aza {{jalink|Shimonishi||Shimonishi}} 50 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori)}} |祭神={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} |社格=former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} 28 ({{jalink|98|Q30575}}) |本殿={{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名=General tutelary of Natori |例祭=May 1 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}}. * Also merged: Kumano Kafuro Kushi Mikenuno-no-Kami, {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} from nearby shrines. === History === Tradition says it was invited by Yamato Takeru in February {{jalink|98|Q30575}} (Keikō 28) during eastern campaign. In November {{jalink|458|Q30922}} (Yūryaku 2), 58 bundles of ritual fields were dedicated and festivals performed. East of the shrine remains the place name “Keiden,” said to derive from those ritual fields. According to shrine history, when Yamato Takeru came to Ōshū for eastern campaign, he fell seriously ill due to successive battles and long travel. In a warm place thick with willows, he built an altar and shrine and prayed for healing, recovered, and safely returned to Yamato. The shrine where those prayers for healing and national peace were made is said to be the origin of Taga Shrine. Place names related to this tradition still remain: * North of Taga Shrine is “Kōdangahara,” where Yamato Takeru set an altar for victory prayers. * “'''Takayanagi'''” (tanayanagi/tanayagi → takayanagi), where a willow altar was made and healing prayed for (current shrine area name). Also, 970 meters southeast of this shrine, on private residential land, stands “Shiratori Shrine” enshrining the spirit of Yamato Takeru. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine precincts File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine File:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine main sanctuary It is said there were frequent disputes with Taga Shrine in Tomizawa (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) over which shrine was the Shikinaisha “Taka Shrine.” The historical work 'Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki' is cited as saying: “Taga Shrine is in Tomizawa Village and now is said to be in Takayanagi Village. However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was formerly called Wakamiya Hachiman and was renamed Taga Shrine.” A dispute also occurred in Meiji 22, resulting in Tomizawa Taga Shrine being treated as the Shikinaisha and Takayanagi Taga Shrine as the site with ritual fields and shrine lands. The place name “'''{{jalink|Nishitaga|Q11627540|Nishitaga}}'''” in Taihaku-ku derives from the Tomizawa shrine, while the Yuriage area and around this shrine in Takayanagi were formerly called “'''{{jalink|Higashitaga|Q11655485|Higashitaga}}'''." In February Meiji 4 it became Gōsha, and later was designated a heihaku offering shrine. It was revered by domain lords and governors of Mutsu. Owing to the Yamato Takeru tradition, it is especially believed beneficial for healing illness and longevity, attracting worshippers not only locally but also from other prefectures. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings (worship hall, heiden, nagare-zukuri main sanctuary) * Precincts also include sacred gate and shrine office. link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''Taga Shrine''' (たがじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in former {{jalink|Natori District|Q6980219}}, {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}. It corresponds to “Taka Shrine,” one of the two shrines in Natori District among the 100 {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' and two current shrines are treated as claim shrines: one in Tomizawa, {{jalink|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Q1043331}}, and one in Takayanagi, {{jalink|Natori|Q642018}}. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku-ku, Sendai) === {{神社 |名称=Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Worship hall of Taga Shrine]]
Worship hall |所在地={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Sendai|Q46747}}{{jalink|Taihaku-ku|Q1043331}}{{jalink|Tomizawa (Sendai)||Tomizawa}} 3-15-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai)}} |祭神={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} |社格=former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} 40 ({{jalink|110|Q30631}}) |本殿=Kasuga-zukuri |別名=Ōtaka-no-miya |例祭=May 1 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deity === * Main deity: {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}. * Also merged are {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} of the Ōnoda area (deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}) and Hōryū Shrine (deity: {{jalink|Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|Takaokami-no-Kami}}). === History === The area where Taga Shrine stands was historically important, containing Jōmon sites such as the Tomizawa and Ōnoda sites, the Ōnoda kofun cluster, and {{jalink|Kōriyama site|Q11643209}}. Taga Shrine is said to be '''the oldest shrine in Sendai'''; according to shrine tradition, it was invited by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} in {{jalink|110|Q30631}} (Keikō 40) during his eastern campaign. In year 2 of the 21st emperor {{jalink|Yūryaku|Q357235}} ({{jalink|458|Q30922}}), it is said to have received 58 bundles of ritual rice field and held divine rites. In ancient times it was reportedly called “Ōtaka-no-miya.” According to the precinct history board, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by local lords and warriors as guardian deity of this region. In the reign of the 70th emperor {{jalink|Go-Reizei|Q356060}}, Chinju-fu shōgun {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}} crossed the {{jalink|Natori River|Q11414763}} during eastern campaign, worshipped here, prayed for lasting military fortune, and dedicated his own handwritten item. A temporary shrine built in the {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} era was heavily damaged. Reverence by successive Sendai lords continued, and with donation from {{jalink|Date Shigemura|Q846378}}, shrine construction was carried out in {{jalink|An'ei}} 4 ({{jalink|1775|Q7700}}). It is also said that {{jalink|Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764}}, lord of Kakuda Castle, offered the divine title “Taga Jinja Izanagi-no-Mikoto,” and the shrine was lively with visits by his wife’s representative and ladies-in-waiting. It was designated Village Shrine in April {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5, merged Kasuga Shrine in Ōnoda and Hōryū Shrine in Higashi-Ōnoda in Meiji 42, and in August {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 8 was designated a heihaku offering shrine. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings (worship hall, heiden, Kasuga-zukuri main sanctuary) * Also stone monuments, stone shrines, hoop-offering place, sacred {{jalink|oak|Q1377319|kashiwa}} tree, kagura hall, and shrine office. In November {{jalink|458|Q30922}} (Yūryaku 2), 58 bundles of ritual fields were dedicated and festivals performed. East of the shrine remains the place name “Keiden,” said to derive from those ritual fields. According to shrine history, when Yamato Takeru came to Ōshū for eastern campaign, he fell seriously ill due to successive battles and long travel. Place names related to this tradition still remain: * North of Taga Shrine is “Kōdangahara,” where Yamato Takeru set an altar for victory prayers. * “'''Takayanagi'''” (tanayanagi/tanayagi → takayanagi), where a willow altar was made and healing prayed for (current shrine area name). Also, 970 meters southeast of this shrine, on private residential land, stands “Shiratori Shrine” enshrining the spirit of Yamato Takeru. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine precincts File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine File:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine main sanctuary It is said there were frequent disputes with Taga Shrine in Tomizawa (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) over which shrine was the Shikinaisha “Taka Shrine.” The historical work 'Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki' is cited as saying: “Taga Shrine is in Tomizawa Village and now is said to be in Takayanagi Village. However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was formerly called Wakamiya Hachiman and was renamed Taga Shrine.” A dispute also occurred in Meiji 22, resulting in Tomizawa Taga Shrine being treated as the Shikinaisha and Takayanagi Taga Shrine as the site with ritual fields and shrine lands. The place name “'''{{jalink|Nishitaga|Q11627540|Nishitaga}}'''” in Taihaku-ku derives from the Tomizawa shrine, while the Yuriage area and around this shrine in Takayanagi were formerly called “'''{{jalink|Higashitaga|Q11655485|Higashitaga}}'''." In February Meiji 4 it became Gōsha, and later was designated a heihaku offering shrine. It was revered by domain lords and governors of Mutsu. Owing to the Yamato Takeru tradition, it is especially believed beneficial for healing illness and longevity, attracting worshippers not only locally but also from other prefectures. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings (worship hall, heiden, nagare-zukuri main sanctuary) * Precincts also include sacred gate and shrine office. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Taga Shrine torii File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Sacred gate File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Worship hall File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Hoops (taga) dedicated at worship hall File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagare-zukuri main sanctuary File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Main sanctuary {{clear|right}} === Bibliography === *Taga Shrine history (August, Taishō 3) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai)] (Miyagi {{jalink|Shrine Office|Q11590703}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori)] (Miyagi {{jalink|Shrine Office|Q11590703}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=宮城県}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''Taga Shrine''' (たがじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in former {{jalink|Natori District|Q6980219}}, {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}. It corresponds to “Taka Shrine,” one of the two shrines in Natori District among the 100 {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' and two current shrines are treated as claim shrines: one in Tomizawa, {{jalink|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Q1043331}}, and one in Takayanagi, {{jalink|Natori|Q642018}}. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku-ku, Sendai) === {{神社 |名称=Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Worship hall of Taga Shrine]]
Worship hall |所在地={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Sendai|Q46747}}{{jalink|Taihaku-ku|Q1043331}}{{jalink|Tomizawa (Sendai)||Tomizawa}} 3-15-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai)}} |祭神={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} |社格=former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} 40 ({{jalink|110|Q30631}}) |本殿=Kasuga-zukuri |別名=Ōtaka-no-miya |例祭=May 1 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deity === * Main deity: {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}. * Also merged are {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} of the Ōnoda area (deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}) and Hōryū Shrine (deity: {{jalink|Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|Takaokami-no-Kami}}). === History === The area where Taga Shrine stands was historically important, containing Jōmon sites such as the Tomizawa and Ōnoda sites, the Ōnoda kofun cluster, and {{jalink|Kōriyama site|Q11643209}}. Taga Shrine is said to be '''the oldest shrine in Sendai'''; according to shrine tradition, it was invited by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} in {{jalink|110|Q30631}} (Keikō 40) during his eastern campaign. In year 2 of the 21st emperor {{jalink|Yūryaku|Q357235}} ({{jalink|458|Q30922}}), it is said to have received 58 bundles of ritual rice field and held divine rites. In ancient times it was reportedly called “Ōtaka-no-miya.” According to the precinct history board, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by local lords and warriors as guardian deity of this region. In the reign of the 70th emperor {{jalink|Go-Reizei|Q356060}}, Chinju-fu shōgun {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}} crossed the {{jalink|Natori River|Q11414763}} during eastern campaign, worshipped here, prayed for lasting military fortune, and dedicated his own handwritten item. A temporary shrine built in the {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} era was heavily damaged. Reverence by successive Sendai lords continued, and with donation from {{jalink|Date Shigemura|Q846378}}, shrine construction was carried out in {{jalink|An'ei}} 4 ({{jalink|1775|Q7700}}). It is also said that {{jalink|Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764}}, lord of Kakuda Castle, offered the divine title “Taga Jinja Izanagi-no-Mikoto,” and the shrine was lively with visits by his wife’s representative and ladies-in-waiting. It was designated Village Shrine in April {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5, merged Kasuga Shrine in Ōnoda and Hōryū Shrine in Higashi-Ōnoda in Meiji 42, and in August {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 8 was designated a heihaku offering shrine. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings (worship hall, heiden, Kasuga-zukuri main sanctuary) * Also stone monuments, stone shrines, hoop-offering place, sacred {{jalink|oak|Q1377319|kashiwa}} tree, kagura hall, and shrine office. File:1st Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 01.JPG|First torii of Taga Shrine File:2nd Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Second torii of Taga Shrine File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Sacred "oak tree" of Taga Shrine File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|In front of worship hall File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|Worship hall File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai ciｔy.JPG|Hoops (taga) dedicated before the deity File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Kasuga-zukuri main sanctuary File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Stone shrine in precincts
Enshrines massha: Yamada-sha, Ko-sha, Kōjin-sha, Kagitori-sha, Meiko-sha, Ise-miya, Hyūga-sha File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|Hoop-offering place
Prayer hoops are dedicated {{-}} === Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori) === {{神社 |名称=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori) |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Worship hall of Taga Shrine]]
Worship hall |所在地={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Natori|Q642018}}{{jalink|Takayanagi (Natori)||Takayanagi}} Aza {{jalink|Shimonishi||Shimonishi}} 50 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori)}} |祭神={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} |社格=former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} 28 ({{jalink|98|Q30575}}) |本殿={{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名=General tutelary of Natori |例祭=May 1 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}}. * Also merged: Kumano Kafuro Kushi Mikenuno-no-Kami, {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} from nearby shrines. === History === Tradition says it was invited by Yamato Takeru in February {{jalink|98|Q30575}} (Keikō 28) during eastern campaign. In November {{jalink|458|Q30922}} (Yūryaku 2), 58 bundles of ritual fields were dedicated and festivals performed. East of the shrine remains the place name “Keiden,” said to derive from those ritual fields. According to shrine history, when Yamato Takeru came to Ōshū for eastern campaign, he fell seriously ill due to successive battles and long travel. In a warm place thick with willows, he built an altar and shrine and prayed for healing, recovered, and safely returned to Yamato. The shrine where those prayers for healing and national peace were made is said to be the origin of Taga Shrine. Place names related to this tradition still remain: * North of Taga Shrine is “Kōdangahara,” where Yamato Takeru set an altar for victory prayers. * “'''Takayanagi'''” (tanayanagi/tanayagi → takayanagi), where a willow altar was made and healing prayed for (current shrine area name). Also, 970 meters southeast of this shrine, on private residential land, stands “Shiratori Shrine” enshrining the spirit of Yamato Takeru. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine precincts File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine File:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Shiratori Shrine main sanctuary It is said there were frequent disputes with Taga Shrine in Tomizawa (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) over which shrine was the Shikinaisha “Taka Shrine.” The historical work 'Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki' is cited as saying: “Taga Shrine is in Tomizawa Village and now is said to be in Takayanagi Village. However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was formerly called Wakamiya Hachiman and was renamed Taga Shrine.” A dispute also occurred in Meiji 22, resulting in Tomizawa Taga Shrine being treated as the Shikinaisha and Takayanagi Taga Shrine as the site with ritual fields and shrine lands. The place name “'''{{jalink|Nishitaga|Q11627540|Nishitaga}}'''” in Taihaku-ku derives from the Tomizawa shrine, while the Yuriage area and around this shrine in Takayanagi were formerly called “'''{{jalink|Higashitaga|Q11655485|Higashitaga}}'''." In February Meiji 4 it became Gōsha, and later was designated a heihaku offering shrine. It was revered by domain lords and governors of Mutsu. Owing to the Yamato Takeru tradition, it is especially believed beneficial for healing illness and longevity, attracting worshippers not only locally but also from other prefectures. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings (worship hall, heiden, nagare-zukuri main sanctuary) * Precincts also include sacred gate and shrine office. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Taga Shrine torii File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Sacred gate File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Worship hall File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Hoops (taga) dedicated at worship hall File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagare-zukuri main sanctuary File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Main sanctuary {{clear|right}} === Bibliography === *Taga Shrine history (August, Taishō 3) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku-ku, Sendai)] (Miyagi {{jalink|Shrine Office|Q11590703}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori)] (Miyagi {{jalink|Shrine Office|Q11590703}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=宮城県}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
