{{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '宮城県仙台市太白区富沢3-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '宮城県名取市高柳', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:多賀神社 (宮城県名取郡)]] {{wikidata link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''多賀神社'''（たがじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|宮城県|Q47896}}旧{{jalink|名取郡|Q6980219}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 {{jalink|陸奥国|Q907389}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式}}{{jalink|式内社}}百座のうち{{jalink|名取郡|Q6980219}}にある二座のうちの一座「多加神社」であり、現在{{jalink|仙台市|Q46747}}{{jalink|太白区|Q1043331}}富沢と{{jalink|名取市|Q642018}}高柳に鎮座する二社が論社とされている。 === 多賀神社（仙台市太白区富沢） === {{神社 |名称=多賀神社（仙台市太白区） |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|多賀神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|宮城県|Q47896}}{{jalink|仙台市|Q46747}}{{jalink|太白区|Q1043331}}{{jalink|富沢 (仙台市)||富沢}}3-15-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=多賀神社（仙台市太白区）}} |祭神={{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}} |社格=旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}
{{jalink|式内社}} |創建={{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}40年（{{jalink|110年|Q30631}}） |本殿=春日造 |別名=大鷹宮 |例祭=5月1日 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=多賀神社|coordinates= {{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === 祭神 === * {{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}}を主祭神として祀る。 * その他に、大野田地区の{{jalink|春日神社|Q10855011}}（祭神：{{jalink|アメノコヤネ||天児屋命}}）と{{jalink|寶龍神社}}（祭神：{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高龗神}}）を合祀している。 ===由緒=== 多賀神社の鎮座する地域には富沢遺跡・大野田遺跡などの縄文時代の遺跡や大野田古墳群、{{jalink|郡山遺跡|Q11643209}}があるなど、歴史的にも重要な地域であった。 多賀神社は'''仙台市内で一番古い神社'''であり、社伝によれば、景行天皇40年（110年）に{{jalink|日本武尊}}が東夷征伐の時に勧請したという。第21代{{jalink|雄略天皇|Q357235}}2年（{{jalink|458年|Q30922}}）には圭田58束を賜り神礼祭式を行ったと伝わる。往古は「大鷹宮」と呼ばれていたという。 境内由緒書によると、多賀神社はこの地方の守護神として領主や武将から篤く崇敬されたという。第70代{{jalink|後冷泉天皇|Q356060}}の御代には、鎮守府将軍である{{jalink|源義家|Q32613}}が東征の折に{{jalink|名取川|Q11414763}}を渡り当社に参拝し、武運長久を祈願し自筆の物を奉納したという。 {{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}年間に建ててあった仮宮が大破。その後も歴代仙台藩主から崇敬されたが、{{jalink|伊達重村|Q846378}}の寄進を受け、{{jalink|安永}}4年（{{jalink|1775年|Q7700}}）に社殿の普請が行われた。角田城主であった{{jalink|石川宗光|Q11585764}}からも「多賀神社伊弉諾尊」の神号献上を受け、奥方の代参や侍女の参詣で神社も賑わったと伝わる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年4月に村社に指定、同42年には大野田地区の春日神社と東大野田地区の寶龍神社を合祀し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}8年の8月には幣帛供進社に指定された。 ===境内=== *社殿（拝殿・幣殿・春日造の本殿） *その他に、石碑や石祠、箍納め所、御神木の{{jalink|カシワ|Q1377319|柏}}の木、神楽殿、社務所がある。 File:1st Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 01.JPG|多賀神社第一鳥居 File:2nd Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|多賀神社第二鳥居 File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|秋の境内 File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|秋の境内 File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|秋の境内 File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|多賀神社の御神木「柏の木」 File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|多賀神社拝殿前 File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|多賀神社拝殿 File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai ciｔy.JPG|神前に奉納されている箍（たが） File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|春日造の多賀神社本殿 File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|多賀神社境内の石祠
末社の山田社、兒社、荒神社、鈎取社、姪子社、伊勢宮、日向社を祀る File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|箍納め所
祈願をこめた箍（たが）が奉納されている {{-}} ===多賀神社（名取市高柳）=== {{神社 |名称=多賀神社（名取市高柳） |画像=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|多賀神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|宮城県|Q47896}}{{jalink|名取市|Q642018}}{{jalink|高柳 (名取市)||高柳}}字{{jalink|高柳下西||下西}}50 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=多賀神社（名取市高柳）}} |祭神={{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}}
{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊弉冉尊}} |社格=旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|式内社}} |創建={{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}28年（{{jalink|98年|Q30575}}） |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名=名取総鎮守 |例祭=5月1日 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=多賀神社|coordinates= {{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} ===祭神=== *{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}}と{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊弉冉尊}}を主祭神として祀る。 *その他に、近隣の神社の祭神である熊野加夫呂櫛御食奴神、{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照皇大神}}、{{jalink|スサノヲ||速須佐男命}}、{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}を合祀している。 ===由緒=== 景行天皇28年2月に日本武尊による東征の折に勧請されたと伝わる。雄略天皇2年(458年)11月に祭祀田として圭田58束を奉り祭礼を行ったという。当社の東には、その折に賜った祭祀田にちなむ「圭田」という地名が残されている。 神社の由緒によれば、日本武尊が東征のために奥州へやってきた時、相次ぐ戦いや長旅によって重い病気を患ってしまった。その時、柳の生い茂る温暖な地で祭壇や祠を設けて病気平癒を祈願したところ回復し、無事に大和の地へ帰ることができたという。その病気平癒や国家安寧を祈願した祠が「多賀神社」のはじまりだと伝わる。 当社の鎮座する地域にはこの由緒にちなむ地域名が現在も残されている。 *多賀神社の北には日本武尊が戦勝祈願のための祭壇を設けた場所である「皇壇原（こうだんがはら）」 *柳の木で祭壇を作り病気平癒を祈願した場所である「'''高柳'''（たなやなぎ（棚柳）→たかやなぎ）」（現在の鎮座地名） また、当社の南東970メートルの民家敷地内には日本武尊の神霊を祀る「白鳥神社」が鎮座している。 File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|白鳥神社境内 File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|白鳥神社 File:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|白鳥神社本殿 当社は仙台市太白区富沢の多賀神社との間で、式内社「多加神社」がどちらの神社であるかという論争が度々起こったという。歴史書「新撰陸奥風土記」には『多賀神社は富沢村にあり今は高柳村に鎮座するという。しかし、高柳の多賀神社は往古は「若宮八幡」と呼ばれていたものを多賀神社へ改称したものである』と書かれているとある。明治22年にも論争が起こり、それにより富沢の多賀神社を式内社、高柳の多賀神社は祭祀田などの社領のあった地だとした。太白区の「'''{{jalink|西多賀村|Q11627540|西多賀}}'''」の地名は富沢の多賀神社にちなむもので、閖上地区や高柳の当社付近は以前は「'''{{jalink|閖上町|Q11655485|東多賀}}'''」と呼ばれていた。 明治4年2月には郷社になり、その後幣帛供進社に指定された。藩主や陸奥国守からも尊崇を集めていた。日本武尊の由緒にちなみ、特に病気平癒、延命長寿に御利益がある神社として近隣地域のみならず他県からも多くの崇敬者を集めている。 ===境内=== *社殿（拝殿・幣殿・流造の本殿） *境内には御神門、社務所がある。 File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|多賀神社鳥居 File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|多賀神社御神門 File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|多賀神社拝殿 File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|多賀神社拝殿に奉納されている箍(たが) File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|流造の多賀神社本殿 File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|多賀神社本殿 {{clear|right}} === 参考文献 === *多賀神社由緒（大正3年8月） === link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''Taga Shrine'' (Taga Shrine) is a {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}} former {{jalink|Natori District|Q6980219}}. {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Engishiki}}{{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} Among the 100 shrines, one of the two in {{jalink|Natori District|Q6980219}} The two shrines currently enshrined in Tomizawa and {{jalink|Natori City|Q642018}} Takayanagi are considered to be Ronsha. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Taga-jinja shrine]]
Oracle |Location={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Sendai City|Q46747}}{{jalink|Taihaku Ward|Q1043331}}{{jalink|Tomizawa (Sendai City)||Tomizawa}}3-15-1 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagison}} |Shakaku=old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}}
{{jalink|shikinaisha}} |Foundation={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}40 years ({{jalink|110 years|Q30631}}) |Main shrine = Kasuga-zukuri |Alternative name=Otakanomiya |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagison}} is enshrined as the main deity. * In addition, {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} (enshrined deity: {{jalink|Amenokoyane||Amagiya-no-Mikoto}}) and {{jalink|Takarayu Shrine}} (enshrined deity: {{jalink|Nakamikami|Q644201|Takaranokami}}) are also enshrined together. ===Venue=== The area where Taga Shrine is located is also a historically important area, as there are Jomon period ruins such as Tomizawa Ruins and Onoda Ruins, the Onoda Tumulus Group, and {{jalink|Koriyama Ruins|Q11643209}}. Taga Shrine is ``the oldest shrine in Sendai City,'' and according to the shrine's legend, it was prayed to by {{jalink|Nihon Takeru}} during his conquest of Toi in the 40th year of Emperor Keiko (110). It is said that in the 21st {{jalink|Emperor Yuryaku|Q357235}} 2nd year ({{jalink|458|Q30922}}), he was given 58 bundles of Keida and held a divine ritual ceremony. It is said that in ancient times it was called ``Otakanomiya''. According to the precinct history book, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by feudal lords and military commanders as the guardian deity of this region. It is said that during the reign of the 70th Emperor Go-Reizei, {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}}, the shogun of the military base, crossed the {{jalink|Natori River|Q11414763}} and worshiped at the shrine during his eastern expedition, praying for good fortune and long life and dedicating something in his own handwriting. The temporary shrine built during {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} was severely damaged. Afterwards, it was revered by successive lords of the Sendai domain, and the construction of the shrine building was carried out in {{jalink|Yasunaga}}4 ({{jalink|1775|Q7700}}) with a donation from {{jalink|Date Shigemura|Q846378}}. It is said that the lord of Kakuda Castle, {{jalink|Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764}}, presented the shrine with the divine name ``Taga Shrine Izanagison,'' and the shrine was crowded with visitors from his wives and maids.{{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} It was designated as a village shrine in April 1950, and in 1942 it was enshrined together with Kasuga Shrine in the Onoda district and Horyu Shrine in the Noda district of Higashidai, and in August 1988 it was designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, Kasuga-zukuri main hall) *In addition, there are stone monuments, stone shrines, shrine shrines, sacred {{jalink|oakashi|Q1377319|oak}} trees, a kagura hall, and a shrine office. File:1st Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 01.JPG|Taga Shrine First Torii File:2nd Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga Shrine second torii gate File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Sacred tree of Taga-jinja shrine "Oakashi" File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|In front of Taga-jinja shrineFile:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga-hoops offered in front of the shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Main shrine of Taga-jinja shrine built in Kasuga style File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts of Taga Shrine
Enshrines the subsidiary shrines Yamada Shrine, Kosha Shrine, Kojin Shrine, Kamatori Shrine, Meiko Shrine, Isemiya Shrine, and Hinata Shrine File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayers
The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer. {{-}} ===Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Taga Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |Location={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Natori City|Q642018}}{{jalink|Takayanagi (Natori City)||Takayanagi}}{{jalink|Takayanagi Shimonishi||Shimonishi}}50 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)}} |Enshrined God={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanami}}
{{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami}} |Shakaku = Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} |Foundation={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}28 ({{jalink|1998|Q30575}}) |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Alternative name = Natori Soushinju |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} ===Enshrined God=== *{{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanamison}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanamison}} are enshrined as the main deities. *In addition, the shrine enshrines the deities of nearby shrines, such as Kumano Kaoro Kushimokukunokami, {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{jalink|Susanowo||Hasusao no Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}. ===Venue=== It is said that he was solicited by Takeru Takeru of Japan in February 28th year of Emperor Keiko's expedition to the east. It is said that in November of the second year of Emperor Yuryaku's reign (458), a festival was held in which 58 bundles of Keida were offered as a ritual field. To the east of our company, there is a place called ``Keida'', which is named after the ritual field that was given to us at that time. According to the history of the shrine, when Takeru Takeru of Japan came to Oshu to conquer the east, he suffered from a serious illness due to a series of battles and long journeys. At that time, he set up an altar and a shrine in a warm area where willows grew and prayed for his recovery, and it is said that he recovered and was able to return safely to the land of Yamato. It is said that the shrine where people prayed for recovery from illness and peace of the nation was the origin of Taga Shrine. The area where our company is located still bears the name of the area associated with this history. *To the north of Taga Shrine is Kodangahara, the place where Takeru Nihon built an altar to pray for victory in battle. *``Tanayanagi'' (Tanayanagi → Takayanagi) (current name of the enshrined place) is the place where an altar was made from a willow tree and people prayed for recovery from illness. Additionally, Shiratori Shrine, which enshrines the spirit of Japan Takeru, is located on the premises of a private house 970 meters southeast of our company. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori-jinja shrineFile:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine It is said that there have been frequent disputes between our company and Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City over which shrine is the Shikinai Shrine, Taka Shrine. The history book ``Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki'' states that ``Taga Shrine was originally located in Tomizawa Village, but is now enshrined in Takayanagi Village.'' However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was originally called ``Wakamiya Hachiman,'' but it was renamed Taga Shrine.'' A controversy arose in 1897, which led to the conclusion that Taga Shrine in Tomizawa was Shikinai shrine, and Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was the land where the shrine's territory, such as ritual fields, was located. The place name ``''{{jalink|Nishitaga Village|Q11627540|Nishitaga}}''' in Taihaku Ward is derived from Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, and the area around our company in Yuriage district and Takayanagi was previously called ``''{{jalink|Yuriage Town|Q11655485|Higashitaga}}'''. It became a local shrine in February 1894, and was later designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. He was respected by the lord of the domain and the governor of Mutsu Province. In connection with the history of Nihon Takeru, the shrine attracts many worshipers not only from nearby areas but also from other prefectures as a shrine that is particularly blessed with healing from illness and extending life. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (Haden of worship, Heiden, Nagaruzo main building) *There is a shrine gate and a shrine office within the precincts. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Taga-hoops dedicated to the worship hall of Taga-jinja Shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagaruzo main hall of Taga-jinja shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine main hall {{clear|right}} === References === *Taga Shrine history (August 1919) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External link === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{jalink|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{jalink|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takashinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Miyagi Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''Taga Shrine'' (Taga Shrine) is a {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}} former {{jalink|Natori District|Q6980219}}. {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Engishiki}}{{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} Among the 100 shrines, one of the two in {{jalink|Natori District|Q6980219}} The two shrines currently enshrined in Tomizawa and {{jalink|Natori City|Q642018}} Takayanagi are considered to be Ronsha. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Taga-jinja shrine]]
Oracle |Location={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Sendai City|Q46747}}{{jalink|Taihaku Ward|Q1043331}}{{jalink|Tomizawa (Sendai City)||Tomizawa}}3-15-1 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagison}} |Shakaku=old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}}
{{jalink|shikinaisha}} |Foundation={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}40 years ({{jalink|110 years|Q30631}}) |Main shrine = Kasuga-zukuri |Alternative name=Otakanomiya |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagison}} is enshrined as the main deity. * In addition, {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} (enshrined deity: {{jalink|Amenokoyane||Amagiya-no-Mikoto}}) and {{jalink|Takarayu Shrine}} (enshrined deity: {{jalink|Nakamikami|Q644201|Takaranokami}}) are also enshrined together. ===Venue=== The area where Taga Shrine is located is also a historically important area, as there are Jomon period ruins such as Tomizawa Ruins and Onoda Ruins, the Onoda Tumulus Group, and {{jalink|Koriyama Ruins|Q11643209}}. Taga Shrine is ``the oldest shrine in Sendai City,'' and according to the shrine's legend, it was prayed to by {{jalink|Nihon Takeru}} during his conquest of Toi in the 40th year of Emperor Keiko (110). It is said that in the 21st {{jalink|Emperor Yuryaku|Q357235}} 2nd year ({{jalink|458|Q30922}}), he was given 58 bundles of Keida and held a divine ritual ceremony. It is said that in ancient times it was called ``Otakanomiya''. According to the precinct history book, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by feudal lords and military commanders as the guardian deity of this region. It is said that during the reign of the 70th Emperor Go-Reizei, {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}}, the shogun of the military base, crossed the {{jalink|Natori River|Q11414763}} and worshiped at the shrine during his eastern expedition, praying for good fortune and long life and dedicating something in his own handwriting. The temporary shrine built during {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} was severely damaged. Afterwards, it was revered by successive lords of the Sendai domain, and the construction of the shrine building was carried out in {{jalink|Yasunaga}}4 ({{jalink|1775|Q7700}}) with a donation from {{jalink|Date Shigemura|Q846378}}. It is said that the lord of Kakuda Castle, {{jalink|Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764}}, presented the shrine with the divine name ``Taga Shrine Izanagison,'' and the shrine was crowded with visitors from his wives and maids.{{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} It was designated as a village shrine in April 1950, and in 1942 it was enshrined together with Kasuga Shrine in the Onoda district and Horyu Shrine in the Noda district of Higashidai, and in August 1988 it was designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, Kasuga-zukuri main hall) *In addition, there are stone monuments, stone shrines, shrine shrines, sacred {{jalink|oakashi|Q1377319|oak}} trees, a kagura hall, and a shrine office. File:1st Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 01.JPG|Taga Shrine First Torii File:2nd Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga Shrine second torii gate File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Sacred tree of Taga-jinja shrine "Oakashi" File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|In front of Taga-jinja shrineFile:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga-hoops offered in front of the shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Main shrine of Taga-jinja shrine built in Kasuga style File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts of Taga Shrine
Enshrines the subsidiary shrines Yamada Shrine, Kosha Shrine, Kojin Shrine, Kamatori Shrine, Meiko Shrine, Isemiya Shrine, and Hinata Shrine File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayers
The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer. {{-}} ===Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Taga Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |Location={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Natori City|Q642018}}{{jalink|Takayanagi (Natori City)||Takayanagi}}{{jalink|Takayanagi Shimonishi||Shimonishi}}50 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)}} |Enshrined God={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanami}}
{{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami}} |Shakaku = Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} |Foundation={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}28 ({{jalink|1998|Q30575}}) |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Alternative name = Natori Soushinju |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} ===Enshrined God=== *{{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanamison}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanamison}} are enshrined as the main deities. *In addition, the shrine enshrines the deities of nearby shrines, such as Kumano Kaoro Kushimokukunokami, {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{jalink|Susanowo||Hasusao no Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}. ===Venue=== It is said that he was solicited by Takeru Takeru of Japan in February 28th year of Emperor Keiko's expedition to the east. It is said that in November of the second year of Emperor Yuryaku's reign (458), a festival was held in which 58 bundles of Keida were offered as a ritual field. To the east of our company, there is a place called ``Keida'', which is named after the ritual field that was given to us at that time. According to the history of the shrine, when Takeru Takeru of Japan came to Oshu to conquer the east, he suffered from a serious illness due to a series of battles and long journeys. At that time, he set up an altar and a shrine in a warm area where willows grew and prayed for his recovery, and it is said that he recovered and was able to return safely to the land of Yamato. It is said that the shrine where people prayed for recovery from illness and peace of the nation was the origin of Taga Shrine. The area where our company is located still bears the name of the area associated with this history. *To the north of Taga Shrine is Kodangahara, the place where Takeru Nihon built an altar to pray for victory in battle. *``Tanayanagi'' (Tanayanagi → Takayanagi) (current name of the enshrined place) is the place where an altar was made from a willow tree and people prayed for recovery from illness. Additionally, Shiratori Shrine, which enshrines the spirit of Japan Takeru, is located on the premises of a private house 970 meters southeast of our company. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori-jinja shrineFile:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine It is said that there have been frequent disputes between our company and Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City over which shrine is the Shikinai Shrine, Taka Shrine. The history book ``Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki'' states that ``Taga Shrine was originally located in Tomizawa Village, but is now enshrined in Takayanagi Village.'' However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was originally called ``Wakamiya Hachiman,'' but it was renamed Taga Shrine.'' A controversy arose in 1897, which led to the conclusion that Taga Shrine in Tomizawa was Shikinai shrine, and Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was the land where the shrine's territory, such as ritual fields, was located. The place name ``''{{jalink|Nishitaga Village|Q11627540|Nishitaga}}''' in Taihaku Ward is derived from Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, and the area around our company in Yuriage district and Takayanagi was previously called ``''{{jalink|Yuriage Town|Q11655485|Higashitaga}}'''. It became a local shrine in February 1894, and was later designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. He was respected by the lord of the domain and the governor of Mutsu Province. In connection with the history of Nihon Takeru, the shrine attracts many worshipers not only from nearby areas but also from other prefectures as a shrine that is particularly blessed with healing from illness and extending life. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (Haden of worship, Heiden, Nagaruzo main building) *There is a shrine gate and a shrine office within the precincts. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Taga-hoops dedicated to the worship hall of Taga-jinja Shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagaruzo main hall of Taga-jinja shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine main hall {{clear|right}} === References === *Taga Shrine history (August 1919) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External link === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{jalink|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{jalink|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takashinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Miyagi Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{jalink|shikinaisha}} |Foundation={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}40 years ({{jalink|110 years|Q30631}}) |Main shrine = Kasuga-zukuri |Alternative name=Otakanomiya |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagison}} is enshrined as the main deity. * In addition, {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} (enshrined deity: {{jalink|Amenokoyane||Amagiya-no-Mikoto}}) and {{jalink|Takarayu Shrine}} (enshrined deity: {{jalink|Nakamikami|Q644201|Takaranokami}}) are also enshrined together. ===Venue=== The area where Taga Shrine is located is also a historically important area, as there are Jomon period ruins such as Tomizawa Ruins and Onoda Ruins, the Onoda Tumulus Group, and {{jalink|Koriyama Ruins|Q11643209}}. Taga Shrine is ``the oldest shrine in Sendai City,'' and according to the shrine's legend, it was prayed to by {{jalink|Nihon Takeru}} during his conquest of Toi in the 40th year of Emperor Keiko (110). It is said that in the 21st {{jalink|Emperor Yuryaku|Q357235}} 2nd year ({{jalink|458|Q30922}}), he was given 58 bundles of Keida and held a divine ritual ceremony. It is said that in ancient times it was called ``Otakanomiya''. According to the precinct history book, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by feudal lords and military commanders as the guardian deity of this region. It is said that during the reign of the 70th Emperor Go-Reizei, {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}}, the shogun of the military base, crossed the {{jalink|Natori River|Q11414763}} and worshiped at the shrine during his eastern expedition, praying for good fortune and long life and dedicating something in his own handwriting. The temporary shrine built during {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} was severely damaged. Afterwards, it was revered by successive lords of the Sendai domain, and the construction of the shrine building was carried out in {{jalink|Yasunaga}}4 ({{jalink|1775|Q7700}}) with a donation from {{jalink|Date Shigemura|Q846378}}. It is said that the lord of Kakuda Castle, {{jalink|Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764}}, presented the shrine with the divine name ``Taga Shrine Izanagison,'' and the shrine was crowded with visitors from his wives and maids.{{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} It was designated as a village shrine in April 1950, and in 1942 it was enshrined together with Kasuga Shrine in the Onoda district and Horyu Shrine in the Noda district of Higashidai, and in August 1988 it was designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, Kasuga-zukuri main hall) *In addition, there are stone monuments, stone shrines, shrine shrines, sacred {{jalink|oakashi|Q1377319|oak}} trees, a kagura hall, and a shrine office. File:1st Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 01.JPG|Taga Shrine First Torii File:2nd Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga Shrine second torii gate File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Sacred tree of Taga-jinja shrine "Oakashi" File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|In front of Taga-jinja shrineFile:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga-hoops offered in front of the shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Main shrine of Taga-jinja shrine built in Kasuga style File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts of Taga Shrine
Enshrines the subsidiary shrines Yamada Shrine, Kosha Shrine, Kojin Shrine, Kamatori Shrine, Meiko Shrine, Isemiya Shrine, and Hinata Shrine File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayers
The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer. {{-}} ===Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Taga Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |Location={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Natori City|Q642018}}{{jalink|Takayanagi (Natori City)||Takayanagi}}{{jalink|Takayanagi Shimonishi||Shimonishi}}50 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)}} |Enshrined God={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanami}}
{{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami}} |Shakaku = Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} |Foundation={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}28 ({{jalink|1998|Q30575}}) |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Alternative name = Natori Soushinju |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} ===Enshrined God=== *{{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanamison}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanamison}} are enshrined as the main deities. *In addition, the shrine enshrines the deities of nearby shrines, such as Kumano Kaoro Kushimokukunokami, {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{jalink|Susanowo||Hasusao no Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}. ===Venue=== It is said that he was solicited by Takeru Takeru of Japan in February 28th year of Emperor Keiko's expedition to the east. It is said that in November of the second year of Emperor Yuryaku's reign (458), a festival was held in which 58 bundles of Keida were offered as a ritual field. To the east of our company, there is a place called ``Keida'', which is named after the ritual field that was given to us at that time. According to the history of the shrine, when Takeru Takeru of Japan came to Oshu to conquer the east, he suffered from a serious illness due to a series of battles and long journeys. At that time, he set up an altar and a shrine in a warm area where willows grew and prayed for his recovery, and it is said that he recovered and was able to return safely to the land of Yamato. It is said that the shrine where people prayed for recovery from illness and peace of the nation was the origin of Taga Shrine. The area where our company is located still bears the name of the area associated with this history. *To the north of Taga Shrine is Kodangahara, the place where Takeru Nihon built an altar to pray for victory in battle. *``Tanayanagi'' (Tanayanagi → Takayanagi) (current name of the enshrined place) is the place where an altar was made from a willow tree and people prayed for recovery from illness. Additionally, Shiratori Shrine, which enshrines the spirit of Japan Takeru, is located on the premises of a private house 970 meters southeast of our company. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori-jinja shrineFile:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine It is said that there have been frequent disputes between our company and Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City over which shrine is the Shikinai Shrine, Taka Shrine. The history book ``Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki'' states that ``Taga Shrine was originally located in Tomizawa Village, but is now enshrined in Takayanagi Village.'' However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was originally called ``Wakamiya Hachiman,'' but it was renamed Taga Shrine.'' A controversy arose in 1897, which led to the conclusion that Taga Shrine in Tomizawa was Shikinai shrine, and Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was the land where the shrine's territory, such as ritual fields, was located. The place name ``''{{jalink|Nishitaga Village|Q11627540|Nishitaga}}''' in Taihaku Ward is derived from Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, and the area around our company in Yuriage district and Takayanagi was previously called ``''{{jalink|Yuriage Town|Q11655485|Higashitaga}}'''. It became a local shrine in February 1894, and was later designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. He was respected by the lord of the domain and the governor of Mutsu Province. In connection with the history of Nihon Takeru, the shrine attracts many worshipers not only from nearby areas but also from other prefectures as a shrine that is particularly blessed with healing from illness and extending life. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (Haden of worship, Heiden, Nagaruzo main building) *There is a shrine gate and a shrine office within the precincts. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Taga-hoops dedicated to the worship hall of Taga-jinja Shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagaruzo main hall of Taga-jinja shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine main hall {{clear|right}} === References === *Taga Shrine history (August 1919) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External link === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{jalink|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{jalink|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takashinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Miyagi Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Engishiki}}{{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} Among the 100 shrines, one of the two in {{jalink|Natori District|Q6980219}} The two shrines currently enshrined in Tomizawa and {{jalink|Natori City|Q642018}} Takayanagi are considered to be Ronsha. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine It is said that the lord of Kakuda Castle, {{jalink|Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764}}, presented the shrine with the divine name ``Taga Shrine Izanagison,'' and the shrine was crowded with visitors from his wives and maids.{{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} It was designated as a village shrine in April 1950, and in 1942 it was enshrined together with Kasuga Shrine in the Onoda district and Horyu Shrine in the Noda district of Higashidai, and in August 1988 it was designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, Kasuga-zukuri main hall) *In addition, there are stone monuments, stone shrines, shrine shrines, sacred {{jalink|oakashi|Q1377319|oak}} trees, a kagura hall, and a shrine office. Enshrines the subsidiary shrines Yamada Shrine, Kosha Shrine, Kojin Shrine, Kamatori Shrine, Meiko Shrine, Isemiya Shrine, and Hinata Shrine File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayers
The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer. {{-}} ===Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Taga Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |Location={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Natori City|Q642018}}{{jalink|Takayanagi (Natori City)||Takayanagi}}{{jalink|Takayanagi Shimonishi||Shimonishi}}50 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)}} |Enshrined God={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanami}}
{{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami}} |Shakaku = Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} |Foundation={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}28 ({{jalink|1998|Q30575}}) |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Alternative name = Natori Soushinju |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} ===Enshrined God=== *{{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanamison}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanamison}} are enshrined as the main deities. *In addition, the shrine enshrines the deities of nearby shrines, such as Kumano Kaoro Kushimokukunokami, {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{jalink|Susanowo||Hasusao no Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}. ===Venue=== It is said that he was solicited by Takeru Takeru of Japan in February 28th year of Emperor Keiko's expedition to the east. It is said that in November of the second year of Emperor Yuryaku's reign (458), a festival was held in which 58 bundles of Keida were offered as a ritual field. To the east of our company, there is a place called ``Keida'', which is named after the ritual field that was given to us at that time. According to the history of the shrine, when Takeru Takeru of Japan came to Oshu to conquer the east, he suffered from a serious illness due to a series of battles and long journeys. At that time, he set up an altar and a shrine in a warm area where willows grew and prayed for his recovery, and it is said that he recovered and was able to return safely to the land of Yamato. It is said that the shrine where people prayed for recovery from illness and peace of the nation was the origin of Taga Shrine. The area where our company is located still bears the name of the area associated with this history. *To the north of Taga Shrine is Kodangahara, the place where Takeru Nihon built an altar to pray for victory in battle. *``Tanayanagi'' (Tanayanagi → Takayanagi) (current name of the enshrined place) is the place where an altar was made from a willow tree and people prayed for recovery from illness. Additionally, Shiratori Shrine, which enshrines the spirit of Japan Takeru, is located on the premises of a private house 970 meters southeast of our company. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori-jinja shrineFile:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine It is said that there have been frequent disputes between our company and Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City over which shrine is the Shikinai Shrine, Taka Shrine. The history book ``Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki'' states that ``Taga Shrine was originally located in Tomizawa Village, but is now enshrined in Takayanagi Village.'' However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was originally called ``Wakamiya Hachiman,'' but it was renamed Taga Shrine.'' A controversy arose in 1897, which led to the conclusion that Taga Shrine in Tomizawa was Shikinai shrine, and Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was the land where the shrine's territory, such as ritual fields, was located. The place name ``''{{jalink|Nishitaga Village|Q11627540|Nishitaga}}''' in Taihaku Ward is derived from Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, and the area around our company in Yuriage district and Takayanagi was previously called ``''{{jalink|Yuriage Town|Q11655485|Higashitaga}}'''. It became a local shrine in February 1894, and was later designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. He was respected by the lord of the domain and the governor of Mutsu Province. In connection with the history of Nihon Takeru, the shrine attracts many worshipers not only from nearby areas but also from other prefectures as a shrine that is particularly blessed with healing from illness and extending life. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (Haden of worship, Heiden, Nagaruzo main building) *There is a shrine gate and a shrine office within the precincts. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Taga-hoops dedicated to the worship hall of Taga-jinja Shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagaruzo main hall of Taga-jinja shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine main hall {{clear|right}} === References === *Taga Shrine history (August 1919) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External link === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{jalink|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{jalink|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takashinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Miyagi Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''Taga Shrine'' (Taga Shrine) is a {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}} former {{jalink|Natori District|Q6980219}}. {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Engishiki}}{{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} Among the 100 shrines, one of the two in {{jalink|Natori District|Q6980219}} The two shrines currently enshrined in Tomizawa and {{jalink|Natori City|Q642018}} Takayanagi are considered to be Ronsha. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Taga-jinja shrine]]
Oracle |Location={{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Sendai City|Q46747}}{{jalink|Taihaku Ward|Q1043331}}{{jalink|Tomizawa (Sendai City)||Tomizawa}}3-15-1 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagison}} |Shakaku=old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}}
{{jalink|shikinaisha}} |Foundation={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}40 years ({{jalink|110 years|Q30631}}) |Main shrine = Kasuga-zukuri |Alternative name=Otakanomiya |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagison}} is enshrined as the main deity. * In addition, {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} (enshrined deity: {{jalink|Amenokoyane||Amagiya-no-Mikoto}}) and {{jalink|Takarayu Shrine}} (enshrined deity: {{jalink|Nakamikami|Q644201|Takaranokami}}) are also enshrined together. ===Venue=== The area where Taga Shrine is located is also a historically important area, as there are Jomon period ruins such as Tomizawa Ruins and Onoda Ruins, the Onoda Tumulus Group, and {{jalink|Koriyama Ruins|Q11643209}}. Taga Shrine is ``the oldest shrine in Sendai City,'' and according to the shrine's legend, it was prayed to by {{jalink|Nihon Takeru}} during his conquest of Toi in the 40th year of Emperor Keiko (110). It is said that in the 21st {{jalink|Emperor Yuryaku|Q357235}} 2nd year ({{jalink|458|Q30922}}), he was given 58 bundles of Keida and held a divine ritual ceremony. It is said that in ancient times it was called ``Otakanomiya''. According to the precinct history book, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by feudal lords and military commanders as the guardian deity of this region. It is said that during the reign of the 70th Emperor Go-Reizei, {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}}, the shogun of the military base, crossed the {{jalink|Natori River|Q11414763}} and worshiped at the shrine during his eastern expedition, praying for good fortune and long life and dedicating something in his own handwriting. The temporary shrine built during {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} was severely damaged. Afterwards, it was revered by successive lords of the Sendai domain, and the construction of the shrine building was carried out in {{jalink|Yasunaga}}4 ({{jalink|1775|Q7700}}) with a donation from {{jalink|Date Shigemura|Q846378}}. It is said that the lord of Kakuda Castle, {{jalink|Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764}}, presented the shrine with the divine name ``Taga Shrine Izanagison,'' and the shrine was crowded with visitors from his wives and maids.{{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} It was designated as a village shrine in April 1950, and in 1942 it was enshrined together with Kasuga Shrine in the Onoda district and Horyu Shrine in the Noda district of Higashidai, and in August 1988 it was designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, Kasuga-zukuri main hall) *In addition, there are stone monuments, stone shrines, shrine shrines, sacred {{jalink|oakashi|Q1377319|oak}} trees, a kagura hall, and a shrine office. To the east of our company, there is a place called ``Keida'', which is named after the ritual field that was given to us at that time. According to the history of the shrine, when Takeru Takeru of Japan came to Oshu to conquer the east, he suffered from a serious illness due to a series of battles and long journeys. At that time, he set up an altar and a shrine in a warm area where willows grew and prayed for his recovery, and it is said that he recovered and was able to return safely to the land of Yamato. It is said that the shrine where people prayed for recovery from illness and peace of the nation was the origin of Taga Shrine. The area where our company is located still bears the name of the area associated with this history. *To the north of Taga Shrine is Kodangahara, the place where Takeru Nihon built an altar to pray for victory in battle. *``Tanayanagi'' (Tanayanagi → Takayanagi) (current name of the enshrined place) is the place where an altar was made from a willow tree and people prayed for recovery from illness. Additionally, Shiratori Shrine, which enshrines the spirit of Japan Takeru, is located on the premises of a private house 970 meters southeast of our company. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori-jinja shrineFile:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine It is said that there have been frequent disputes between our company and Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City over which shrine is the Shikinai Shrine, Taka Shrine. The history book ``Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki'' states that ``Taga Shrine was originally located in Tomizawa Village, but is now enshrined in Takayanagi Village.'' However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was originally called ``Wakamiya Hachiman,'' but it was renamed Taga Shrine.'' A controversy arose in 1897, which led to the conclusion that Taga Shrine in Tomizawa was Shikinai shrine, and Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was the land where the shrine's territory, such as ritual fields, was located. The place name ``''{{jalink|Nishitaga Village|Q11627540|Nishitaga}}''' in Taihaku Ward is derived from Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, and the area around our company in Yuriage district and Takayanagi was previously called ``''{{jalink|Yuriage Town|Q11655485|Higashitaga}}'''. It became a local shrine in February 1894, and was later designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. He was respected by the lord of the domain and the governor of Mutsu Province. In connection with the history of Nihon Takeru, the shrine attracts many worshipers not only from nearby areas but also from other prefectures as a shrine that is particularly blessed with healing from illness and extending life. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (Haden of worship, Heiden, Nagaruzo main building) *There is a shrine gate and a shrine office within the precincts. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Taga-hoops dedicated to the worship hall of Taga-jinja Shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagaruzo main hall of Taga-jinja shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine main hall {{clear|right}} === References === *Taga Shrine history (August 1919) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External link === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{jalink|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{jalink|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takashinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Miyagi Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]]

link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''Taga Shrine'' (Taga Shrine) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}} former {{ill|lt=Natori District|Q6980219|WD=Q6980219}}. {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}{{ill||lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|UNKNOWN}} Among the 100 shrines, one of the two in {{ill|lt=Natori District|Q6980219|WD=Q6980219}} The two shrines currently enshrined in Tomizawa and {{ill|lt=Natori City|Q642018|WD=Q642018}} Takayanagi are considered to be Ronsha. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Taga-jinja shrine]]
Oracle |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Sendai City|Q46747|WD=Q46747}}{{ill|lt=Taihaku Ward|Q1043331|WD=Q1043331}}{{ill|lt=Tomizawa (Sendai City)||WD=|lt=Tomizawa}}3-15-1 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagison}} |Shakaku=old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}
{{ill||lt=shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}40 years ({{ill|lt=110 years|Q30631|WD=Q30631}}) |Main shrine = Kasuga-zukuri |Alternative name=Otakanomiya |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagison}} is enshrined as the main deity. * In addition, {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011}} (enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amenokoyane||WD=|lt=Amagiya-no-Mikoto}}) and {{ill||lt=Takarayu Shrine|UNKNOWN}} (enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Nakamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaranokami}}) are also enshrined together. ===Venue=== The area where Taga Shrine is located is also a historically important area, as there are Jomon period ruins such as Tomizawa Ruins and Onoda Ruins, the Onoda Tumulus Group, and {{ill|lt=Koriyama Ruins|Q11643209|WD=Q11643209}}. Taga Shrine is ``the oldest shrine in Sendai City,'' and according to the shrine's legend, it was prayed to by {{ill||lt=Nihon Takeru|UNKNOWN}} during his conquest of Toi in the 40th year of Emperor Keiko (110). It is said that in the 21st {{ill|lt=Emperor Yuryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=458|Q30922|WD=Q30922}}), he was given 58 bundles of Keida and held a divine ritual ceremony. It is said that in ancient times it was called ``Otakanomiya''. According to the precinct history book, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by feudal lords and military commanders as the guardian deity of this region. It is said that during the reign of the 70th Emperor Go-Reizei, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|WD=Q32613}}, the shogun of the military base, crossed the {{ill|lt=Natori River|Q11414763|WD=Q11414763}} and worshiped at the shrine during his eastern expedition, praying for good fortune and long life and dedicating something in his own handwriting. The temporary shrine built during {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} was severely damaged. Afterwards, it was revered by successive lords of the Sendai domain, and the construction of the shrine building was carried out in {{ill||lt=Yasunaga|UNKNOWN}}4 ({{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700}}) with a donation from {{ill|lt=Date Shigemura|Q846378|WD=Q846378}}. It is said that the lord of Kakuda Castle, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764|WD=Q11585764}}, presented the shrine with the divine name ``Taga Shrine Izanagison,'' and the shrine was crowded with visitors from his wives and maids.{{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} It was designated as a village shrine in April 1950, and in 1942 it was enshrined together with Kasuga Shrine in the Onoda district and Horyu Shrine in the Noda district of Higashidai, and in August 1988 it was designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, Kasuga-zukuri main hall) *In addition, there are stone monuments, stone shrines, shrine shrines, sacred {{ill|lt=oakashi|Q1377319|WD=Q1377319|lt=oak}} trees, a kagura hall, and a shrine office. Enshrines the subsidiary shrines Yamada Shrine, Kosha Shrine, Kojin Shrine, Kamatori Shrine, Meiko Shrine, Isemiya Shrine, and Hinata Shrine File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayers

It is said that in November of the second year of Emperor Yuryaku's reign (458), a festival was held in which 58 bundles of Keida were offered as a ritual field. To the east of our company, there is a place called ``Keida'', which is named after the ritual field that was given to us at that time. According to the history of the shrine, when Takeru Takeru of Japan came to Oshu to conquer the east, he suffered from a serious illness due to a series of battles and long journeys. At that time, he set up an altar and a shrine in a warm area where willows grew and prayed for his recovery, and it is said that he recovered and was able to return safely to the land of Yamato. It is said that the shrine where people prayed for recovery from illness and peace of the nation was the origin of Taga Shrine. The area where our company is located still bears the name of the area associated with this history. *To the north of Taga Shrine is Kodangahara, the place where Takeru Nihon built an altar to pray for victory in battle. *``Tanayanagi'' (Tanayanagi → Takayanagi) (current name of the enshrined place) is the place where an altar was made from a willow tree and people prayed for recovery from illness. Additionally, Shiratori Shrine, which enshrines the spirit of Japan Takeru, is located on the premises of a private house 970 meters southeast of our company. The history book ``Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki'' states that ``Taga Shrine was originally located in Tomizawa Village, but is now enshrined in Takayanagi Village.'' However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was originally called ``Wakamiya Hachiman,'' but it was renamed Taga Shrine.'' A controversy arose in 1897, which led to the conclusion that Taga Shrine in Tomizawa was Shikinai shrine, and Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was the land where the shrine's territory, such as ritual fields, was located. The place name ``''{{ill|lt=Nishitaga Village|Q11627540|WD=Q11627540|lt=Nishitaga}}''' in Taihaku Ward is derived from Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, and the area around our company in Yuriage district and Takayanagi was previously called ``''{{ill|lt=Yuriage Town|Q11655485|WD=Q11655485|lt=Higashitaga}}'''. It became a local shrine in February 1894, and was later designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. He was respected by the lord of the domain and the governor of Mutsu Province. In connection with the history of Nihon Takeru, the shrine attracts many worshipers not only from nearby areas but also from other prefectures as a shrine that is particularly blessed with healing from illness and extending life. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (Haden of worship, Heiden, Nagaruzo main building) *There is a shrine gate and a shrine office within the precincts. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Taga-hoops dedicated to the worship hall of Taga-jinja Shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagaruzo main hall of Taga-jinja shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine main hall {{clear|right}} === References === *Taga Shrine history (August 1919) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External link === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703}}) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takashinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Miyagi Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]]

link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''Taga Shrine'' (Taga Shrine) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}} former {{ill|lt=Natori District|Q6980219|WD=Q6980219}}. {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|UNKNOWN}} Among the 100 shrines, one of the two in {{ill|lt=Natori District|Q6980219|WD=Q6980219}} The two shrines currently enshrined in Tomizawa and {{ill|lt=Natori City|Q642018|WD=Q642018}} Takayanagi are considered to be Ronsha. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Taga-jinja shrine]]
Oracle |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Sendai City|Q46747|WD=Q46747}}{{ill|lt=Taihaku Ward|Q1043331|WD=Q1043331}}{{ill|lt=Tomizawa (Sendai City)||WD=|lt=Tomizawa}}3-15-1 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagison}} |Shakaku=old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}40 years ({{ill|lt=110 years|Q30631|WD=Q30631}}) |Main shrine = Kasuga-zukuri |Alternative name=Otakanomiya |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagison}} is enshrined as the main deity. * In addition, {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011}} (enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amenokoyane||WD=|lt=Amagiya-no-Mikoto}}) and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takarayu Shrine|UNKNOWN}} (enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Nakamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaranokami}}) are also enshrined together. ===Venue=== The area where Taga Shrine is located is also a historically important area, as there are Jomon period ruins such as Tomizawa Ruins and Onoda Ruins, the Onoda Tumulus Group, and {{ill|lt=Koriyama Ruins|Q11643209|WD=Q11643209}}. Taga Shrine is ``the oldest shrine in Sendai City,'' and according to the shrine's legend, it was prayed to by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nihon Takeru|UNKNOWN}} during his conquest of Toi in the 40th year of Emperor Keiko (110). It is said that in the 21st {{ill|lt=Emperor Yuryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=458|Q30922|WD=Q30922}}), he was given 58 bundles of Keida and held a divine ritual ceremony. It is said that in ancient times it was called ``Otakanomiya''. According to the precinct history book, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by feudal lords and military commanders as the guardian deity of this region. It is said that during the reign of the 70th Emperor Go-Reizei, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|WD=Q32613}}, the shogun of the military base, crossed the {{ill|lt=Natori River|Q11414763|WD=Q11414763}} and worshiped at the shrine during his eastern expedition, praying for good fortune and long life and dedicating something in his own handwriting. The temporary shrine built during {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} was severely damaged. Afterwards, it was revered by successive lords of the Sendai domain, and the construction of the shrine building was carried out in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasunaga|UNKNOWN}}4 ({{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700}}) with a donation from {{ill|lt=Date Shigemura|Q846378|WD=Q846378}}. It is said that the lord of Kakuda Castle, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764|WD=Q11585764}}, presented the shrine with the divine name ``Taga Shrine Izanagison,'' and the shrine was crowded with visitors from his wives and maids.{{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} It was designated as a village shrine in April 1950, and in 1942 it was enshrined together with Kasuga Shrine in the Onoda district and Horyu Shrine in the Noda district of Higashidai, and in August 1988 it was designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, Kasuga-zukuri main hall) *In addition, there are stone monuments, stone shrines, shrine shrines, sacred {{ill|lt=oakashi|Q1377319|WD=Q1377319|lt=oak}} trees, a kagura hall, and a shrine office. File:1st Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 01.JPG|Taga Shrine First Torii File:2nd Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga Shrine second torii gate File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Sacred tree of Taga-jinja shrine "Oakashi" File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|In front of Taga-jinja shrineFile:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga-hoops offered in front of the shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Main shrine of Taga-jinja shrine built in Kasuga style File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts of Taga Shrine
Enshrines the subsidiary shrines Yamada Shrine, Kosha Shrine, Kojin Shrine, Kamatori Shrine, Meiko Shrine, Isemiya Shrine, and Hinata Shrine File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayers
The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer. {{-}} ===Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Taga Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Natori City|Q642018|WD=Q642018}}{{ill|lt=Takayanagi (Natori City)||WD=|lt=Takayanagi}}{{ill|lt=Takayanagi Shimonishi||WD=|lt=Shimonishi}}50 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)}} |Enshrined God={{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanami}}
{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} |Shakaku = Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|UNKNOWN}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}28 ({{ill|lt=1998|Q30575|WD=Q30575}}) |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternative name = Natori Soushinju |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} ===Enshrined God=== *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanamison}} and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanamison}} are enshrined as the main deities. *In addition, the shrine enshrines the deities of nearby shrines, such as Kumano Kaoro Kushimokukunokami, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanowo||WD=|lt=Hasusao no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}. ===Venue=== It is said that he was solicited by Takeru Takeru of Japan in February 28th year of Emperor Keiko's expedition to the east. It is said that in November of the second year of Emperor Yuryaku's reign (458), a festival was held in which 58 bundles of Keida were offered as a ritual field. To the east of our company, there is a place called ``Keida'', which is named after the ritual field that was given to us at that time. According to the history of the shrine, when Takeru Takeru of Japan came to Oshu to conquer the east, he suffered from a serious illness due to a series of battles and long journeys. At that time, he set up an altar and a shrine in a warm area where willows grew and prayed for his recovery, and it is said that he recovered and was able to return safely to the land of Yamato. It is said that the shrine where people prayed for recovery from illness and peace of the nation was the origin of Taga Shrine. The area where our company is located still bears the name of the area associated with this history. *To the north of Taga Shrine is Kodangahara, the place where Takeru Nihon built an altar to pray for victory in battle. *``Tanayanagi'' (Tanayanagi → Takayanagi) (current name of the enshrined place) is the place where an altar was made from a willow tree and people prayed for recovery from illness. Additionally, Shiratori Shrine, which enshrines the spirit of Japan Takeru, is located on the premises of a private house 970 meters southeast of our company. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori-jinja shrineFile:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine It is said that there have been frequent disputes between our company and Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City over which shrine is the Shikinai Shrine, Taka Shrine. The history book ``Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki'' states that ``Taga Shrine was originally located in Tomizawa Village, but is now enshrined in Takayanagi Village.'' However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was originally called ``Wakamiya Hachiman,'' but it was renamed Taga Shrine.'' A controversy arose in 1897, which led to the conclusion that Taga Shrine in Tomizawa was Shikinai shrine, and Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was the land where the shrine's territory, such as ritual fields, was located. The place name ``''{{ill|lt=Nishitaga Village|Q11627540|WD=Q11627540|lt=Nishitaga}}''' in Taihaku Ward is derived from Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, and the area around our company in Yuriage district and Takayanagi was previously called ``''{{ill|lt=Yuriage Town|Q11655485|WD=Q11655485|lt=Higashitaga}}'''. It became a local shrine in February 1894, and was later designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. He was respected by the lord of the domain and the governor of Mutsu Province. In connection with the history of Nihon Takeru, the shrine attracts many worshipers not only from nearby areas but also from other prefectures as a shrine that is particularly blessed with healing from illness and extending life. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (Haden of worship, Heiden, Nagaruzo main building) *There is a shrine gate and a shrine office within the precincts. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Taga-hoops dedicated to the worship hall of Taga-jinja Shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagaruzo main hall of Taga-jinja shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine main hall {{clear|right}} === References === *Taga Shrine history (August 1919) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External link === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703}}) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takashinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Miyagi Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]]
