At that time, he set up an altar and a shrine in a warm area where willows grew and prayed for his recovery, and it is said that he recovered and was able to return safely to the land of Yamato. It is said that the shrine where people prayed for recovery from illness and peace of the nation was the origin of Taga Shrine. The area where our company is located still bears the name of the area associated with this history. *To the north of Taga Shrine is Kodangahara, the place where Takeru Nihon built an altar to pray for victory in battle. *``Tanayanagi'' (Tanayanagi → Takayanagi) (current name of the enshrined place) is the place where an altar was made from a willow tree and people prayed for recovery from illness. Additionally, Shiratori Shrine, which enshrines the spirit of Japan Takeru, is located on the premises of a private house 970 meters southeast of our company. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori-jinja shrineFile:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine It is said that there have been frequent disputes between our company and Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City over which shrine is the Shikinai Shrine, Taka Shrine. The history book ``Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki'' states that ``Taga Shrine was originally located in Tomizawa Village, but is now enshrined in Takayanagi Village.'' However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was originally called ``Wakamiya Hachiman,'' but it was renamed Taga Shrine.'' A controversy arose in 1897, which led to the conclusion that Taga Shrine in Tomizawa was Shikinai shrine, and Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was the land where the shrine's territory, such as ritual fields, was located. The place name ``''{{ill|lt=Nishitaga Village|Q11627540|WD=Q11627540|lt=Nishitaga}}''' in Taihaku Ward is derived from Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, and the area around our company in Yuriage district and Takayanagi was previously called ``''{{ill|lt=Yuriage Town|Q11655485|WD=Q11655485|lt=Higashitaga}}'''. It became a local shrine in February 1894, and was later designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. He was respected by the lord of the domain and the governor of Mutsu Province. In connection with the history of Nihon Takeru, the shrine attracts many worshipers not only from nearby areas but also from other prefectures as a shrine that is particularly blessed with healing from illness and extending life. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (Haden of worship, Heiden, Nagaruzo main building) *There is a shrine gate and a shrine office within the precincts. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Taga-hoops dedicated to the worship hall of Taga-jinja Shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagaruzo main hall of Taga-jinja shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine main hall {{clear|right}} === References === *Taga Shrine history (August 1919) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External link === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703}}) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takashinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Miyagi Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]]

(宮城県名取郡)]] {{wikidata link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''Taga Shrine'' (Taga Shrine) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} former {{ill|lt=Natori District|Q6980219|WD=Q6980219|1=Natori District, Miyagi}}. {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}} Among the 100 shrines, one of the two in {{ill|lt=Natori District|Q6980219|WD=Q6980219|1=Natori District, Miyagi}} The two shrines currently enshrined in Tomizawa and {{ill|lt=Natori City|Q642018|WD=Q642018|1=Natori, Miyagi}} Takayanagi are considered to be Ronsha. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Taga-jinja shrine]]
Oracle |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sendai City|Q46747|WD=Q46747|1=Sendai}}{{ill|lt=Taihaku Ward|Q1043331|WD=Q1043331|1=Taihaku-ku, Sendai}}{{ill|lt=Tomizawa (Sendai City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tomizawa}}3-15-1 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagison|1=Izanagi}} |Shakaku=old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}40 years ({{ill|lt=110 years|Q30631|WD=Q30631|1=AD 110}}) |Main shrine = Kasuga-zukuri |Alternative name=Otakanomiya |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagison|1=Izanagi}} is enshrined as the main deity. * In addition, {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} (enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagiya-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}) and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takarayu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Nakamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaranokami|1=Kuraokami}}) are also enshrined together. ===Venue=== The area where Taga Shrine is located is also a historically important area, as there are Jomon period ruins such as Tomizawa Ruins and Onoda Ruins, the Onoda Tumulus Group, and {{ill|lt=Koriyama Ruins|Q11643209|WD=Q11643209|1=Sendai Kōriyama Kanga ruins}}. Taga Shrine is ``the oldest shrine in Sendai City,'' and according to the shrine's legend, it was prayed to by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nihon Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} during his conquest of Toi in the 40th year of Emperor Keiko (110). It is said that in the 21st {{ill|lt=Emperor Yuryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=458|Q30922|WD=Q30922|1=458}}), he was given 58 bundles of Keida and held a divine ritual ceremony. It is said that in ancient times it was called ``Otakanomiya''. According to the precinct history book, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by feudal lords and military commanders as the guardian deity of this region. It is said that during the reign of the 70th Emperor Go-Reizei, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|WD=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, the shogun of the military base, crossed the {{ill|lt=Natori River|Q11414763|WD=Q11414763|1=Natori River}} and worshiped at the shrine during his eastern expedition, praying for good fortune and long life and dedicating something in his own handwriting. The temporary shrine built during {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} was severely damaged. Afterwards, it was revered by successive lords of the Sendai domain, and the construction of the shrine building was carried out in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasunaga|1=UNKNOWN}}4 ({{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700|1=1775}}) with a donation from {{ill|lt=Date Shigemura|Q846378|WD=Q846378|1=Date Shigemura}}. It is said that the lord of Kakuda Castle, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764|WD=Q11585764|1=Ishikawa Munemitsu}}, presented the shrine with the divine name ``Taga Shrine Izanagison,'' and the shrine was crowded with visitors from his wives and maids.{{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} It was designated as a village shrine in April 1950, and in 1942 it was enshrined together with Kasuga Shrine in the Onoda district and Horyu Shrine in the Noda district of Higashidai, and in August 1988 it was designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, Kasuga-zukuri main hall) *In addition, there are stone monuments, stone shrines, shrine shrines, sacred {{ill|lt=oakashi|Q1377319|WD=Q1377319|lt=oak|1=Quercus dentata}} trees, a kagura hall, and a shrine office. File:1st Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 01.JPG|Taga Shrine First Torii File:2nd Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga Shrine second torii gate File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Sacred tree of Taga-jinja shrine "Oakashi" File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|In front of Taga-jinja shrineFile:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga-hoops offered in front of the shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Main shrine of Taga-jinja shrine built in Kasuga style File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts of Taga Shrine
Enshrines the subsidiary shrines Yamada Shrine, Kosha Shrine, Kojin Shrine, Kamatori Shrine, Meiko Shrine, Isemiya Shrine, and Hinata Shrine File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayers
The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer. {{-}} ===Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Taga Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Natori City|Q642018|WD=Q642018|1=Natori, Miyagi}}{{ill|lt=Takayanagi (Natori City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayanagi}}{{ill|lt=Takayanagi Shimonishi|UNKNOWN|lt=Shimonishi}}50 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)}} |Enshrined God={{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanami|1=Izanagi}}
{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} |Shakaku = Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}28 ({{ill|lt=1998|Q30575|WD=Q30575|1=AD 98}}) |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = Natori Soushinju |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} ===Enshrined God=== *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanami}} are enshrined as the main deities. *In addition, the shrine enshrines the deities of nearby shrines, such as Kumano Kaoro Kushimokukunokami, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanowo|UNKNOWN|lt=Hasusao no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. ===Venue=== It is said that he was solicited by Takeru Takeru of Japan in February 28th year of Emperor Keiko's expedition to the east. It is said that in November of the second year of Emperor Yuryaku's reign (458), a festival was held in which 58 bundles of Keida were offered as a ritual field. To the east of our company, there is a place called ``Keida'', which is named after the ritual field that was given to us at that time. According to the history of the shrine, when Takeru Takeru of Japan came to Oshu to conquer the east, he suffered from a serious illness due to a series of battles and long journeys. At that time, he set up an altar and a shrine in a warm area where willows grew and prayed for his recovery, and it is said that he recovered and was able to return safely to the land of Yamato. It is said that the shrine where people prayed for recovery from illness and peace of the nation was the origin of Taga Shrine. The area where our company is located still bears the name of the area associated with this history. *To the north of Taga Shrine is Kodangahara, the place where Takeru Nihon built an altar to pray for victory in battle. *``Tanayanagi'' (Tanayanagi → Takayanagi) (current name of the enshrined place) is the place where an altar was made from a willow tree and people prayed for recovery from illness. Additionally, Shiratori Shrine, which enshrines the spirit of Japan Takeru, is located on the premises of a private house 970 meters southeast of our company. The history book ``Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki'' states that ``Taga Shrine was originally located in Tomizawa Village, but is now enshrined in Takayanagi Village.'' However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was originally called ``Wakamiya Hachiman,'' but it was renamed Taga Shrine.'' A controversy arose in 1897, which led to the conclusion that Taga Shrine in Tomizawa was Shikinai shrine, and Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was the land where the shrine's territory, such as ritual fields, was located. The place name ``''{{ill|lt=Nishitaga Village|Q11627540|WD=Q11627540|lt=Nishitaga|1=Nishitaga}}''' in Taihaku Ward is derived from Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, and the area around our company in Yuriage district and Takayanagi was previously called ``''{{ill|lt=Yuriage Town|Q11655485|WD=Q11655485|lt=Higashitaga|1=Yuriage}}'''. It became a local shrine in February 1894, and was later designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. He was respected by the lord of the domain and the governor of Mutsu Province. In connection with the history of Nihon Takeru, the shrine attracts many worshipers not only from nearby areas but also from other prefectures as a shrine that is particularly blessed with healing from illness and extending life. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (Haden of worship, Heiden, Nagaruzo main building) *There is a shrine gate and a shrine office within the precincts. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Taga-hoops dedicated to the worship hall of Taga-jinja Shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagaruzo main hall of Taga-jinja shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine main hall {{clear|right}} === References === *Taga Shrine history (August 1919) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External link === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takashinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Miyagi Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]]

Shrine|WD=Q111303544|1=Taga Shrine}} == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039984|ltq=Q135039984|WD=Q135039984|1=Takano Shrine (Q135039984)}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: (宮城県名取郡)]] {{wikidata link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''Taga Shrine'' (Taga Shrine) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} former {{ill|lt=Natori District|Q6980219|WD=Q6980219|1=Natori District, Miyagi}}. {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}} Among the 100 shrines, one of the two in {{ill|lt=Natori District|Q6980219|WD=Q6980219|1=Natori District, Miyagi}} The two shrines currently enshrined in Tomizawa and {{ill|lt=Natori City|Q642018|WD=Q642018|1=Natori, Miyagi}} Takayanagi are considered to be Ronsha. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Taga-jinja shrine]]
Oracle |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sendai City|Q46747|WD=Q46747|1=Sendai}}{{ill|lt=Taihaku Ward|Q1043331|WD=Q1043331|1=Taihaku-ku, Sendai}}{{ill|lt=Tomizawa (Sendai City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tomizawa}}3-15-1 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagison|1=Izanagi}} |Shakaku=old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}40 years ({{ill|lt=110 years|Q30631|WD=Q30631|1=AD 110}}) |Main shrine = Kasuga-zukuri |Alternative name=Otakanomiya |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagison|1=Izanagi}} is enshrined as the main deity. * In addition, {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} (enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagiya-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}) and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takarayu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Nakamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaranokami|1=Kuraokami}}) are also enshrined together. ===Venue=== The area where Taga Shrine is located is also a historically important area, as there are Jomon period ruins such as Tomizawa Ruins and Onoda Ruins, the Onoda Tumulus Group, and {{ill|lt=Koriyama Ruins|Q11643209|WD=Q11643209|1=Sendai Kōriyama Kanga ruins}}. Taga Shrine is ``the oldest shrine in Sendai City,'' and according to the shrine's legend, it was prayed to by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nihon Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} during his conquest of Toi in the 40th year of Emperor Keiko (110). It is said that in the 21st {{ill|lt=Emperor Yuryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=458|Q30922|WD=Q30922|1=458}}), he was given 58 bundles of Keida and held a divine ritual ceremony. It is said that in ancient times it was called ``Otakanomiya''. According to the precinct history book, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by feudal lords and military commanders as the guardian deity of this region. It is said that during the reign of the 70th Emperor Go-Reizei, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|WD=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, the shogun of the military base, crossed the {{ill|lt=Natori River|Q11414763|WD=Q11414763|1=Natori River}} and worshiped at the shrine during his eastern expedition, praying for good fortune and long life and dedicating something in his own handwriting. The temporary shrine built during {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} was severely damaged. Afterwards, it was revered by successive lords of the Sendai domain, and the construction of the shrine building was carried out in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasunaga|1=UNKNOWN}}4 ({{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700|1=1775}}) with a donation from {{ill|lt=Date Shigemura|Q846378|WD=Q846378|1=Date Shigemura}}. It is said that the lord of Kakuda Castle, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764|WD=Q11585764|1=Ishikawa Munemitsu}}, presented the shrine with the divine name ``Taga Shrine Izanagison,'' and the shrine was crowded with visitors from his wives and maids.{{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} It was designated as a village shrine in April 1950, and in 1942 it was enshrined together with Kasuga Shrine in the Onoda district and Horyu Shrine in the Noda district of Higashidai, and in August 1988 it was designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, Kasuga-zukuri main hall) *In addition, there are stone monuments, stone shrines, shrine shrines, sacred {{ill|lt=oakashi|Q1377319|WD=Q1377319|lt=oak|1=Quercus dentata}} trees, a kagura hall, and a shrine office. File:1st Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 01.JPG|Taga Shrine First Torii File:2nd Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga Shrine second torii gate File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Sacred tree of Taga-jinja shrine "Oakashi" File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|In front of Taga-jinja shrineFile:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga-hoops offered in front of the shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Main shrine of Taga-jinja shrine built in Kasuga style File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts of Taga Shrine
Enshrines the subsidiary shrines Yamada Shrine, Kosha Shrine, Kojin Shrine, Kamatori Shrine, Meiko Shrine, Isemiya Shrine, and Hinata Shrine File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayers
The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer. {{-}} ===Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Taga Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Natori City|Q642018|WD=Q642018|1=Natori, Miyagi}}{{ill|lt=Takayanagi (Natori City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayanagi}}{{ill|lt=Takayanagi Shimonishi|UNKNOWN|lt=Shimonishi}}50 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)}} |Enshrined God={{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanami|1=Izanagi}}
{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} |Shakaku = Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}28 ({{ill|lt=1998|Q30575|WD=Q30575|1=AD 98}}) |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = Natori Soushinju |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} ===Enshrined God=== *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanami}} are enshrined as the main deities. *In addition, the shrine enshrines the deities of nearby shrines, such as Kumano Kaoro Kushimokukunokami, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanowo|UNKNOWN|lt=Hasusao no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. ===Venue=== It is said that he was solicited by Takeru Takeru of Japan in February 28th year of Emperor Keiko's expedition to the east. It is said that in November of the second year of Emperor Yuryaku's reign (458), a festival was held in which 58 bundles of Keida were offered as a ritual field. To the east of our company, there is a place called ``Keida'', which is named after the ritual field that was given to us at that time. According to the history of the shrine, when Takeru Takeru of Japan came to Oshu to conquer the east, he suffered from a serious illness due to a series of battles and long journeys. At that time, he set up an altar and a shrine in a warm area where willows grew and prayed for his recovery, and it is said that he recovered and was able to return safely to the land of Yamato. It is said that the shrine where people prayed for recovery from illness and peace of the nation was the origin of Taga Shrine. The area where our company is located still bears the name of the area associated with this history. *To the north of Taga Shrine is Kodangahara, the place where Takeru Nihon built an altar to pray for victory in battle. *``Tanayanagi'' (Tanayanagi → Takayanagi) (current name of the enshrined place) is the place where an altar was made from a willow tree and people prayed for recovery from illness. Additionally, Shiratori Shrine, which enshrines the spirit of Japan Takeru, is located on the premises of a private house 970 meters southeast of our company. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori-jinja shrineFile:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine It is said that there have been frequent disputes between our company and Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City over which shrine is the Shikinai Shrine, Taka Shrine. The history book ``Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki'' states that ``Taga Shrine was originally located in Tomizawa Village, but is now enshrined in Takayanagi Village.'' However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was originally called ``Wakamiya Hachiman,'' but it was renamed Taga Shrine.'' A controversy arose in 1897, which led to the conclusion that Taga Shrine in Tomizawa was Shikinai shrine, and Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was the land where the shrine's territory, such as ritual fields, was located. The place name ``''{{ill|lt=Nishitaga Village|Q11627540|WD=Q11627540|lt=Nishitaga|1=Nishitaga}}''' in Taihaku Ward is derived from Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, and the area around our company in Yuriage district and Takayanagi was previously called ``''{{ill|lt=Yuriage Town|Q11655485|WD=Q11655485|lt=Higashitaga|1=Yuriage}}'''. It became a local shrine in February 1894, and was later designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. He was respected by the lord of the domain and the governor of Mutsu Province. In connection with the history of Nihon Takeru, the shrine attracts many worshipers not only from nearby areas but also from other prefectures as a shrine that is particularly blessed with healing from illness and extending life. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (Haden of worship, Heiden, Nagaruzo main building) *There is a shrine gate and a shrine office within the precincts. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Taga-hoops dedicated to the worship hall of Taga-jinja Shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagaruzo main hall of Taga-jinja shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine main hall {{clear|right}} === References === *Taga Shrine history (August 1919) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External link === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takashinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Miyagi Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]]

Shrine|WD=Q111303544|1=Taga Shrine}} == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039984|ltq=Q135039984|WD=Q135039984|1=Takano Shrine (Q135039984)}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '宮城県仙台市太白区富沢3-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '宮城県名取市高柳', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:多賀神社 (宮城県名取郡)]] {{wikidata link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''Taga Shrine'' (Taga Shrine) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} former {{ill|lt=Natori District|Q6980219|WD=Q6980219|1=Natori District, Miyagi}}. {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}} Among the 100 shrines, one of the two in {{ill|lt=Natori District|Q6980219|WD=Q6980219|1=Natori District, Miyagi}} The two shrines currently enshrined in Tomizawa and {{ill|lt=Natori City|Q642018|WD=Q642018|1=Natori, Miyagi}} Takayanagi are considered to be Ronsha. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Taga-jinja shrine]]
Oracle |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sendai City|Q46747|WD=Q46747|1=Sendai}}{{ill|lt=Taihaku Ward|Q1043331|WD=Q1043331|1=Taihaku-ku, Sendai}}{{ill|lt=Tomizawa (Sendai City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tomizawa}}3-15-1 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagison|1=Izanagi}} |Shakaku=old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}40 years ({{ill|lt=110 years|Q30631|WD=Q30631|1=AD 110}}) |Main shrine = Kasuga-zukuri |Alternative name=Otakanomiya |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagison|1=Izanagi}} is enshrined as the main deity. * In addition, {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} (enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagiya-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}) and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takarayu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Nakamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaranokami|1=Kuraokami}}) are also enshrined together. ===Venue=== The area where Taga Shrine is located is also a historically important area, as there are Jomon period ruins such as Tomizawa Ruins and Onoda Ruins, the Onoda Tumulus Group, and {{ill|lt=Koriyama Ruins|Q11643209|WD=Q11643209|1=Sendai Kōriyama Kanga ruins}}. Taga Shrine is ``the oldest shrine in Sendai City,'' and according to the shrine's legend, it was prayed to by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nihon Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} during his conquest of Toi in the 40th year of Emperor Keiko (110). It is said that in the 21st {{ill|lt=Emperor Yuryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=458|Q30922|WD=Q30922|1=458}}), he was given 58 bundles of Keida and held a divine ritual ceremony. It is said that in ancient times it was called ``Otakanomiya''. According to the precinct history book, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by feudal lords and military commanders as the guardian deity of this region. It is said that during the reign of the 70th Emperor Go-Reizei, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|WD=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, the shogun of the military base, crossed the {{ill|lt=Natori River|Q11414763|WD=Q11414763|1=Natori River}} and worshiped at the shrine during his eastern expedition, praying for good fortune and long life and dedicating something in his own handwriting. The temporary shrine built during {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} was severely damaged. Afterwards, it was revered by successive lords of the Sendai domain, and the construction of the shrine building was carried out in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasunaga|1=UNKNOWN}}4 ({{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700|1=1775}}) with a donation from {{ill|lt=Date Shigemura|Q846378|WD=Q846378|1=Date Shigemura}}. It is said that the lord of Kakuda Castle, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764|WD=Q11585764|1=Ishikawa Munemitsu}}, presented the shrine with the divine name ``Taga Shrine Izanagison,'' and the shrine was crowded with visitors from his wives and maids.{{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} It was designated as a village shrine in April 1950, and in 1942 it was enshrined together with Kasuga Shrine in the Onoda district and Horyu Shrine in the Noda district of Higashidai, and in August 1988 it was designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, Kasuga-zukuri main hall) *In addition, there are stone monuments, stone shrines, shrine shrines, sacred {{ill|lt=oakashi|Q1377319|WD=Q1377319|lt=oak|1=Quercus dentata}} trees, a kagura hall, and a shrine office. File:1st Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 01.JPG|Taga Shrine First Torii File:2nd Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga Shrine second torii gate File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Sacred tree of Taga-jinja shrine "Oakashi" File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|In front of Taga-jinja shrineFile:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga-hoops offered in front of the shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Main shrine of Taga-jinja shrine built in Kasuga style File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts of Taga Shrine
Enshrines the subsidiary shrines Yamada Shrine, Kosha Shrine, Kojin Shrine, Kamatori Shrine, Meiko Shrine, Isemiya Shrine, and Hinata Shrine File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayers
The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer. {{-}} ===Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Taga Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Natori City|Q642018|WD=Q642018|1=Natori, Miyagi}}{{ill|lt=Takayanagi (Natori City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayanagi}}{{ill|lt=Takayanagi Shimonishi|UNKNOWN|lt=Shimonishi}}50 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)}} |Enshrined God={{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanami|1=Izanagi}}
{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} |Shakaku = Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}28 ({{ill|lt=1998|Q30575|WD=Q30575|1=AD 98}}) |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = Natori Soushinju |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} ===Enshrined God=== *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanami}} are enshrined as the main deities. *In addition, the shrine enshrines the deities of nearby shrines, such as Kumano Kaoro Kushimokukunokami, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanowo|UNKNOWN|lt=Hasusao no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. ===Venue=== It is said that he was solicited by Takeru Takeru of Japan in February 28th year of Emperor Keiko's expedition to the east. It is said that in November of the second year of Emperor Yuryaku's reign (458), a festival was held in which 58 bundles of Keida were offered as a ritual field. To the east of our company, there is a place called ``Keida'', which is named after the ritual field that was given to us at that time. According to the history of the shrine, when Takeru Takeru of Japan came to Oshu to conquer the east, he suffered from a serious illness due to a series of battles and long journeys. At that time, he set up an altar and a shrine in a warm area where willows grew and prayed for his recovery, and it is said that he recovered and was able to return safely to the land of Yamato. It is said that the shrine where people prayed for recovery from illness and peace of the nation was the origin of Taga Shrine. The area where our company is located still bears the name of the area associated with this history. *To the north of Taga Shrine is Kodangahara, the place where Takeru Nihon built an altar to pray for victory in battle. *``Tanayanagi'' (Tanayanagi → Takayanagi) (current name of the enshrined place) is the place where an altar was made from a willow tree and people prayed for recovery from illness. Additionally, Shiratori Shrine, which enshrines the spirit of Japan Takeru, is located on the premises of a private house 970 meters southeast of our company. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori-jinja shrineFile:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine It is said that there have been frequent disputes between our company and Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City over which shrine is the Shikinai Shrine, Taka Shrine. The history book ``Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki'' states that ``Taga Shrine was originally located in Tomizawa Village, but is now enshrined in Takayanagi Village.'' However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was originally called ``Wakamiya Hachiman,'' but it was renamed Taga Shrine.'' A controversy arose in 1897, which led to the conclusion that Taga Shrine in Tomizawa was Shikinai shrine, and Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was the land where the shrine's territory, such as ritual fields, was located. The place name ``''{{ill|lt=Nishitaga Village|Q11627540|WD=Q11627540|lt=Nishitaga|1=Nishitaga}}''' in Taihaku Ward is derived from Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, and the area around our company in Yuriage district and Takayanagi was previously called ``''{{ill|lt=Yuriage Town|Q11655485|WD=Q11655485|lt=Higashitaga|1=Yuriage}}'''. It became a local shrine in February 1894, and was later designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. He was respected by the lord of the domain and the governor of Mutsu Province. In connection with the history of Nihon Takeru, the shrine attracts many worshipers not only from nearby areas but also from other prefectures as a shrine that is particularly blessed with healing from illness and extending life. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (Haden of worship, Heiden, Nagaruzo main building) *There is a shrine gate and a shrine office within the precincts. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Taga-hoops dedicated to the worship hall of Taga-jinja Shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagaruzo main hall of Taga-jinja shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine main hall {{clear|right}} === References === *Taga Shrine history (August 1919) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External link === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Miyagi Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]]

Shrine|WD=Q111303544|1=Taga Shrine}} == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039984|ltq=Q135039984|WD=Q135039984|1=Takano Shrine (Q135039984)}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '宮城県仙台市太白区富沢3-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '宮城県名取市高柳', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:多賀神社 (宮城県名取郡)]] {{wikidata link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''Taga Shrine'' (Taga Shrine) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} former {{ill|lt=Natori District|Q6980219|WD=Q6980219|1=Natori District, Miyagi}}. {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}} Among the 100 shrines, one of the two in {{ill|lt=Natori District|Q6980219|WD=Q6980219|1=Natori District, Miyagi}} The two shrines currently enshrined in Tomizawa and {{ill|lt=Natori City|Q642018|WD=Q642018|1=Natori, Miyagi}} Takayanagi are considered to be Ronsha. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Taga-jinja shrine]]
Oracle |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sendai City|Q46747|WD=Q46747|1=Sendai}}{{ill|lt=Taihaku Ward|Q1043331|WD=Q1043331|1=Taihaku-ku, Sendai}}{{ill|lt=Tomizawa (Sendai City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tomizawa}}3-15-1 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagison|1=Izanagi}} |Shakaku=old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}40 years ({{ill|lt=110 years|Q30631|WD=Q30631|1=AD 110}}) |Main shrine = Kasuga-zukuri |Alternative name=Otakanomiya |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagison|1=Izanagi}} is enshrined as the main deity. * In addition, {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} (enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagiya-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}) and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takarayu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Nakamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaranokami|1=Kuraokami}}) are also enshrined together. ===Venue=== The area where Taga Shrine is located is also a historically important area, as there are Jomon period ruins such as Tomizawa Ruins and Onoda Ruins, the Onoda Tumulus Group, and {{ill|lt=Koriyama Ruins|Q11643209|WD=Q11643209|1=Sendai Kōriyama Kanga ruins}}. Taga Shrine is ``the oldest shrine in Sendai City,'' and according to the shrine's legend, it was prayed to by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nihon Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} during his conquest of Toi in the 40th year of Emperor Keiko (110). It is said that in the 21st {{ill|lt=Emperor Yuryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=458|Q30922|WD=Q30922|1=458}}), he was given 58 bundles of Keida and held a divine ritual ceremony. It is said that in ancient times it was called ``Otakanomiya''. According to the precinct history book, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by feudal lords and military commanders as the guardian deity of this region. It is said that during the reign of the 70th Emperor Go-Reizei, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|WD=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, the shogun of the military base, crossed the {{ill|lt=Natori River|Q11414763|WD=Q11414763|1=Natori River}} and worshiped at the shrine during his eastern expedition, praying for good fortune and long life and dedicating something in his own handwriting. The temporary shrine built during {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} was severely damaged. Afterwards, it was revered by successive lords of the Sendai domain, and the construction of the shrine building was carried out in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasunaga|1=UNKNOWN}}4 ({{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700|1=1775}}) with a donation from {{ill|lt=Date Shigemura|Q846378|WD=Q846378|1=Date Shigemura}}. It is said that the lord of Kakuda Castle, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764|WD=Q11585764|1=Ishikawa Munemitsu}}, presented the shrine with the divine name ``Taga Shrine Izanagison,'' and the shrine was crowded with visitors from his wives and maids.{{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} It was designated as a village shrine in April 1950, and in 1942 it was enshrined together with Kasuga Shrine in the Onoda district and Horyu Shrine in the Noda district of Higashidai, and in August 1988 it was designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, Kasuga-zukuri main hall) *In addition, there are stone monuments, stone shrines, shrine shrines, sacred {{ill|lt=oakashi|Q1377319|WD=Q1377319|lt=oak|1=Quercus dentata}} trees, a kagura hall, and a shrine office. File:1st Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 01.JPG|Taga Shrine First Torii File:2nd Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga Shrine second torii gate File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Sacred tree of Taga-jinja shrine "Oakashi" File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|In front of Taga-jinja shrineFile:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga-hoops offered in front of the shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Main shrine of Taga-jinja shrine built in Kasuga style File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts of Taga Shrine
Enshrines the subsidiary shrines Yamada Shrine, Kosha Shrine, Kojin Shrine, Kamatori Shrine, Meiko Shrine, Isemiya Shrine, and Hinata Shrine File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayers
The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer. {{-}} ===Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Taga Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Natori City|Q642018|WD=Q642018|1=Natori, Miyagi}}{{ill|lt=Takayanagi (Natori City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayanagi}}{{ill|lt=Takayanagi Shimonishi|UNKNOWN|lt=Shimonishi}}50 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)}} |Enshrined God={{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanami|1=Izanagi}}
{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} |Shakaku = Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}28 ({{ill|lt=1998|Q30575|WD=Q30575|1=AD 98}}) |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = Natori Soushinju |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} ===Enshrined God=== *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanami}} are enshrined as the main deities. *In addition, the shrine enshrines the deities of nearby shrines, such as Kumano Kaoro Kushimokukunokami, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanowo|UNKNOWN|lt=Hasusao no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. ===Venue=== It is said that he was solicited by Takeru Takeru of Japan in February 28th year of Emperor Keiko's expedition to the east. It is said that in November of the second year of Emperor Yuryaku's reign (458), a festival was held in which 58 bundles of Keida were offered as a ritual field. To the east of our company, there is a place called ``Keida'', which is named after the ritual field that was given to us at that time. According to the history of the shrine, when Takeru Takeru of Japan came to Oshu to conquer the east, he suffered from a serious illness due to a series of battles and long journeys. At that time, he set up an altar and a shrine in a warm area where willows grew and prayed for his recovery, and it is said that he recovered and was able to return safely to the land of Yamato. It is said that the shrine where people prayed for recovery from illness and peace of the nation was the origin of Taga Shrine. The area where our company is located still bears the name of the area associated with this history. *To the north of Taga Shrine is Kodangahara, the place where Takeru Nihon built an altar to pray for victory in battle. *``Tanayanagi'' (Tanayanagi → Takayanagi) (current name of the enshrined place) is the place where an altar was made from a willow tree and people prayed for recovery from illness. Additionally, Shiratori Shrine, which enshrines the spirit of Japan Takeru, is located on the premises of a private house 970 meters southeast of our company. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori-jinja shrineFile:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine It is said that there have been frequent disputes between our company and Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City over which shrine is the Shikinai Shrine, Taka Shrine. The history book ``Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki'' states that ``Taga Shrine was originally located in Tomizawa Village, but is now enshrined in Takayanagi Village.'' However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was originally called ``Wakamiya Hachiman,'' but it was renamed Taga Shrine.'' A controversy arose in 1897, which led to the conclusion that Taga Shrine in Tomizawa was Shikinai shrine, and Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was the land where the shrine's territory, such as ritual fields, was located. The place name ``''{{ill|lt=Nishitaga Village|Q11627540|WD=Q11627540|lt=Nishitaga|1=Nishitaga}}''' in Taihaku Ward is derived from Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, and the area around our company in Yuriage district and Takayanagi was previously called ``''{{ill|lt=Yuriage Town|Q11655485|WD=Q11655485|lt=Higashitaga|1=Yuriage}}'''. It became a local shrine in February 1894, and was later designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. He was respected by the lord of the domain and the governor of Mutsu Province. In connection with the history of Nihon Takeru, the shrine attracts many worshipers not only from nearby areas but also from other prefectures as a shrine that is particularly blessed with healing from illness and extending life. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (Haden of worship, Heiden, Nagaruzo main building) *There is a shrine gate and a shrine office within the precincts. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Taga-hoops dedicated to the worship hall of Taga-jinja Shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagaruzo main hall of Taga-jinja shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine main hall {{clear|right}} === References === *Taga Shrine history (August 1919) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External link === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Miyagi Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:多賀神社]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]]

Shrine|WD=Q111303544|1=Taga Shrine}} == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039984|ltq=Q135039984|WD=Q135039984|1=Takano Shrine (Q135039984)}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '宮城県仙台市太白区富沢3-15-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '宮城県名取市高柳', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:多賀神社 (宮城県名取郡)]] {{wikidata link|Q11431092}} == Japanese content == '''Taga Shrine'' (Taga Shrine) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} former {{ill|lt=Natori District|Q6980219|WD=Q6980219|1=Natori District, Miyagi}}. {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}} Among the 100 shrines, one of the two in {{ill|lt=Natori District|Q6980219|WD=Q6980219|1=Natori District, Miyagi}} The two shrines currently enshrined in Tomizawa and {{ill|lt=Natori City|Q642018|WD=Q642018|1=Natori, Miyagi}} Takayanagi are considered to be Ronsha. === Taga Shrine (Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 3.JPG|270px|Taga-jinja shrine]]
Oracle |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sendai City|Q46747|WD=Q46747|1=Sendai}}{{ill|lt=Taihaku Ward|Q1043331|WD=Q1043331|1=Taihaku-ku, Sendai}}{{ill|lt=Tomizawa (Sendai City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tomizawa}}3-15-1 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|region:JP-04_type_landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagison|1=Izanagi}} |Shakaku=old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}40 years ({{ill|lt=110 years|Q30631|WD=Q30631|1=AD 110}}) |Main shrine = Kasuga-zukuri |Alternative name=Otakanomiya |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|13|4.6|N|140|51|34.6|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagison|1=Izanagi}} is enshrined as the main deity. * In addition, {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} (enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagiya-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}) and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takarayu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Nakamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaranokami|1=Kuraokami}}) are also enshrined together. ===Venue=== The area where Taga Shrine is located is also a historically important area, as there are Jomon period ruins such as Tomizawa Ruins and Onoda Ruins, the Onoda Tumulus Group, and {{ill|lt=Koriyama Ruins|Q11643209|WD=Q11643209|1=Sendai Kōriyama Kanga ruins}}. Taga Shrine is ``the oldest shrine in Sendai City,'' and according to the shrine's legend, it was prayed to by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nihon Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} during his conquest of Toi in the 40th year of Emperor Keiko (110). It is said that in the 21st {{ill|lt=Emperor Yuryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=458|Q30922|WD=Q30922|1=458}}), he was given 58 bundles of Keida and held a divine ritual ceremony. It is said that in ancient times it was called ``Otakanomiya''. According to the precinct history book, Taga Shrine was deeply revered by feudal lords and military commanders as the guardian deity of this region. It is said that during the reign of the 70th Emperor Go-Reizei, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|WD=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, the shogun of the military base, crossed the {{ill|lt=Natori River|Q11414763|WD=Q11414763|1=Natori River}} and worshiped at the shrine during his eastern expedition, praying for good fortune and long life and dedicating something in his own handwriting. The temporary shrine built during {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} was severely damaged. Afterwards, it was revered by successive lords of the Sendai domain, and the construction of the shrine building was carried out in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasunaga|1=UNKNOWN}}4 ({{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700|1=1775}}) with a donation from {{ill|lt=Date Shigemura|Q846378|WD=Q846378|1=Date Shigemura}}. It is said that the lord of Kakuda Castle, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Munemitsu|Q11585764|WD=Q11585764|1=Ishikawa Munemitsu}}, presented the shrine with the divine name ``Taga Shrine Izanagison,'' and the shrine was crowded with visitors from his wives and maids.{{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} It was designated as a village shrine in April 1950, and in 1942 it was enshrined together with Kasuga Shrine in the Onoda district and Horyu Shrine in the Noda district of Higashidai, and in August 1988 it was designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, Kasuga-zukuri main hall) *In addition, there are stone monuments, stone shrines, shrine shrines, sacred {{ill|lt=oakashi|Q1377319|WD=Q1377319|lt=oak|1=Quercus dentata}} trees, a kagura hall, and a shrine office. File:1st Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 01.JPG|Taga Shrine First Torii File:2nd Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga Shrine second torii gate File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 1.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 2.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward, Sendai city 3.JPG|Autumn precincts File:Daimyo oak tree of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Sacred tree of Taga-jinja shrine "Oakashi" File:Precincts of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|In front of Taga-jinja shrineFile:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Taga-hoops offered in front of the shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Main shrine of Taga-jinja shrine built in Kasuga style File:Stone shrine of Taga-jinja shrine in Taihaku ward,Sendai city.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts of Taga Shrine
Enshrines the subsidiary shrines Yamada Shrine, Kosha Shrine, Kojin Shrine, Kamatori Shrine, Meiko Shrine, Isemiya Shrine, and Hinata Shrine File:The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer.JPG|The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayers
The shrine offering Taga-hoops with prayer. {{-}} ===Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) === {{Shrine |Name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|270px|Taga Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Natori City|Q642018|WD=Q642018|1=Natori, Miyagi}}{{ill|lt=Takayanagi (Natori City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayanagi}}{{ill|lt=Takayanagi Shimonishi|UNKNOWN|lt=Shimonishi}}50 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|name=Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)}} |Enshrined God={{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanami|1=Izanagi}}
{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} |Shakaku = Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}28 ({{ill|lt=1998|Q30575|WD=Q30575|1=AD 98}}) |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = Natori Soushinju |Regular festival = May 1st |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Taga Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|10|37.5|N|140|54|48.2|E}}}} }} ===Enshrined God=== *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanami}} are enshrined as the main deities. *In addition, the shrine enshrines the deities of nearby shrines, such as Kumano Kaoro Kushimokukunokami, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanowo|UNKNOWN|lt=Hasusao no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. ===Venue=== It is said that he was solicited by Takeru Takeru of Japan in February 28th year of Emperor Keiko's expedition to the east. It is said that in November of the second year of Emperor Yuryaku's reign (458), a festival was held in which 58 bundles of Keida were offered as a ritual field. To the east of our company, there is a place called ``Keida'', which is named after the ritual field that was given to us at that time. According to the history of the shrine, when Takeru Takeru of Japan came to Oshu to conquer the east, he suffered from a serious illness due to a series of battles and long journeys. At that time, he set up an altar and a shrine in a warm area where willows grew and prayed for his recovery, and it is said that he recovered and was able to return safely to the land of Yamato. It is said that the shrine where people prayed for recovery from illness and peace of the nation was the origin of Taga Shrine. The area where our company is located still bears the name of the area associated with this history. *To the north of Taga Shrine is Kodangahara, the place where Takeru Nihon built an altar to pray for victory in battle. *``Tanayanagi'' (Tanayanagi → Takayanagi) (current name of the enshrined place) is the place where an altar was made from a willow tree and people prayed for recovery from illness. Additionally, Shiratori Shrine, which enshrines the spirit of Japan Takeru, is located on the premises of a private house 970 meters southeast of our company. File:Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Precincts of Shiratori-jinja shrine File:Shiratori-jinja shrine at Natori city.JPG|Shiratori-jinja shrineFile:Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Honden of Shiratori-jinja shrine It is said that there have been frequent disputes between our company and Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City over which shrine is the Shikinai Shrine, Taka Shrine. The history book ``Shinsen Mutsu Fudoki'' states that ``Taga Shrine was originally located in Tomizawa Village, but is now enshrined in Takayanagi Village.'' However, Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was originally called ``Wakamiya Hachiman,'' but it was renamed Taga Shrine.'' A controversy arose in 1897, which led to the conclusion that Taga Shrine in Tomizawa was Shikinai shrine, and Taga Shrine in Takayanagi was the land where the shrine's territory, such as ritual fields, was located. The place name ``''{{ill|lt=Nishitaga Village|Q11627540|WD=Q11627540|lt=Nishitaga|1=Nishitaga}}''' in Taihaku Ward is derived from Taga Shrine in Tomizawa, and the area around our company in Yuriage district and Takayanagi was previously called ``''{{ill|lt=Yuriage Town|Q11655485|WD=Q11655485|lt=Higashitaga|1=Yuriage}}'''. It became a local shrine in February 1894, and was later designated as a Heihaku Kushinsha. He was respected by the lord of the domain and the governor of Mutsu Province. In connection with the history of Nihon Takeru, the shrine attracts many worshipers not only from nearby areas but also from other prefectures as a shrine that is particularly blessed with healing from illness and extending life. ===Precincts=== *Shrine building (Haden of worship, Heiden, Nagaruzo main building) *There is a shrine gate and a shrine office within the precincts. File:Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Torii gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 01.JPG|Shinmon gate of Taga-jinja shrine File:Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Haiden of Taga-jinja shrine File:Taga-hoops of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city.JPG|Taga-hoops dedicated to the worship hall of Taga-jinja Shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine at Natori city.jpg|Nagaruzo main hall of Taga-jinja shrine File:Honden of Taga-jinja shrine in Natori city 2.JPG|Taga-jinja shrine main hall {{clear|right}} === References === *Taga Shrine history (August 1919) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External link === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010026 Taga Shrine (Taihaku Ward, Sendai City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010291 Taga Shrine (Takayanagi, Natori City)] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Miyagi Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:名取市の歴史]] [[Category:Taga Shrines]] [[Category:名取市の建築物]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:太白区の建築物]] [[Category:太白区の歴史]]
